default_value.help: default value to display when the requested attribute is not available

warn_when_unavailable.help: Whether or not to issue a warning when the requested attribute is unavailable

primitive_attribute_name.help: the name of primitive attribute to display when attribute 'map type' is set to 'primitive attribute'

primitive_attribute_type.help: the type of primitive attribute to display when attribute 'map type' is set to 'primitive attribute'

color.help: input color - preferably a connected map

axis_space.help: the space of the axis to rotate about

angle.help: the angle of rotation in degrees

output_space.help: the space to transform the resulting vector to

mb_shutter_open.help: Frame at which the shutter opens, i.e., the beginning of the motion blur interval.

mb_shutter_close.help: Frame at which the shutter closes, i.e., the end of the motion blur interval.

mb_shutter_bias.help: Biases the motion blur samples toward one end of the shutter interval.

pixel_sample_map.help: Map indicating the number of pixel samples that should be used per pixel (in uniform sampling mode). This is a multiplier on the global pixel sample count specified in SceneVariables. If the provided map has incompatible dimensions, it will be resized.

medium_material.help: The material the camera is 'inside'. If no medium_geometry is specified, ALL rays will have this initial index of refraction applied.

medium_geometry.help: The geometry the camera is 'inside' to which you'd like the medium_material applied. (The use case for this is typically partially-submerged cameras)

uv_attribute.help: Specifies a Vec2f primitive attribute to use as the uv coordinates.  If empty, the default uv for the mesh is used.  The uvs must provide a unique parameterization of the mesh, i.e. a given (u, v) can appear only once on the mesh being baked.

bias.help: Ray-tracing offset for primary ray origin

use_relative_bias.help: If true, bias is scaled based on position magnitude

map_factor.help: Increase or decrease the internal position map buffer resolution

normal_map.help: Use this option to supply your own normals that are used when computing ray directions.  Without this option, normals are computed from the geometry and do not take into account any material applied normal mapping.

normal_map_space.help: Use camera space if you generated per frame normal maps in a pre-pass using the normal material aov.  You probably want to use tangent space if you are using a normal map that is also used in the surfacing setup.

projector_type.help: The projection type used to map points to the flap opening. Perspective has a focal point, while orthographic does not.

projector_focal_distance.help: The distance of the flap opening from the projector origin. Ignored for orthographic projection.

projector_width.help: The width of the frustum at distance 1.0.

projector_height.help: The height of the frustum at distance 1.0.

pre_barn_mode.help: Force the region before the pre_barn_distance to be fully filtered (black), not filtered at all (white), or treated the same as elsewhere (default).

pre_barn_distance.help: The distance from the BarnDoorLightFilter that the pre_barn_mode control takes effect.

edge_scale_top.help: The scale factor for the top edge.

edge_scale_bottom.help: The scale factor for the bottom edge.

edge_scale_left.help: The scale factor for the left edge.

edge_scale_right.help: The scale factor for the right edge.

density.help: Fades the filter effect. 0 means no effect (like having no filter), and 1 means full effect.

invert.help: Swap application of the filter from inside the Barn Door to outside.

radius.help: The radius by which to convert the base box shape into a rounded box, as a proportion of half the width (or height, whichever is smaller).

edge.help: The size of the transition zone from the rounded box to the outside, as a proportion of width (or height, whichever is smaller).

mode.help: Analytical mode allows light to shading points that project to the flap opening. Physical mode allows light whose direction goes through the flap opening.

size_top.help: Additional size on the top edge.

size_bottom.help: Additional size on the bottom edge.

size_left.help: Additional size on the left edge.

size_right.help: Additional size on the right edge.

use_light_xform.help: Attach the filter to the light (in the -Z direction) and ignore node_xform.

rotation.help: The angle to rotate the Barn Door counter-clockwise as seen from the light, in degrees.

color.help: Color within the Barn Door lit region. For each color channel 0 is full shadow and 1 is no shadow.

label.help: label used in light aovs

bake_resolution_mode.help:
Method to specify grid resolution of baked density grid.  Choices are:
		"default": For shaders that are bound to vdb volumes, use vdb resolution.
						For shaders that are bounds to mesh geometries use 100 divisions
		"divisions": Specify number of divisions.
		"voxel size": Specify voxel size.

bake_divisions.help: Divide widest axis by this many divisions

bake_voxel_size.help: Size of voxel in world space

surface_opacity_threshold.help: Accumulated opacity that's considered the 'surface' for computing surface position and Z

diffuse_color.help: Reflectance color of the volume. Technically this is called scattering albedo, which is the scattering coefficient divided by the extinction coefficient.

use_diffuse_ramp.help: Use a ramp to define different diffuse colors depending on the depth of the volume.

diffuse_min_depth.help: Represents the minimum ray depth, or the shortest visible distance a ray has to travel through the volume. This sets the lower bound for the ramp.

diffuse_max_depth.help: Represents the maximum ray depth, or the longest visible distance a ray has to travel through the volume. This sets the upper bound for the ramp.

anisotropy.help: Value in the interval [-1,1] that defines how foward (1) or backward (-1) scattering the volume is.  A value of 0.0 indicates an isotropic volume.

attenuation_color.help: A color to tint (multiply to) the attenuation. Technically the product of attenuation color and intensity is the attenuation (extinction) coefficient.(Note the inverse behavior of color with this parameter.)

attenuation_intensity.help: The rate at which the light traversing a volume is attenuated. The attenuation (extinction) coefficient is the product of attenuation_color, attenuation_intensity, and attenuation_factor

attenuation_factor.help: Identical in behavior to attenuation_intensity but provided as a second means  to control attenuation, intended for use during lighting as a per-shot or  per-sequence adjustment.

match_diffuse.help: Use the same color(s) for attenuation that is/are being used for diffuse.

use_attenuation_ramp.help: Use a ramp to define different attenuation colors depending on the depth of the volume.

attenuation_min_depth.help: Represents the minimum ray depth, or the shortest visible distance a ray has to travel through the volume. This sets the lower bound for the ramp.

attenuation_max_depth.help: Represents the maximum ray depth, or the longest visible distance a ray has to travel through the volume. This sets the upper bound for the ramp.

emission_intensity.help: The rate at which a volume emits light at a given point.  The product of emission color and intensity is the emission coefficient.

emission_color.help: A color multiplier for the emission.  The product of emission color and intensity is the emission coefficient

density_min_depth.help: Represents the minimum ray depth, or the shortest visible distance a ray has to travel through the volume. This sets the lower bound for the ramp.

density_max_depth.help: Represents the maximum ray depth, or the longest visible distance a ray has to travel through the volume. This sets the upper bound for the ramp.

blendAmt.help: Float between [0,1] determining amount of blend between input1 and input2

blendType.help: Method used to blend between input1 and input2.

input1.help: First RenderOutput to use in the blend operation

input2.help: Second RenderOutput to use in the blend operation

mask.help: RenderOutput used to mask the output, revealing input1

invert_mask.help: Invert the value of the mask

color_A.help: The color you get if blend amount is 0

color_B.help: The color you get if blend amount is 1

blend_amount.help: The amount to blend between color A (0) and color B (1)

threshold_min.help: If the blend amount is less than or equal to this amount, it will choose color A (0)

threshold_max.help: If the blend amount is greater than this amount, it will choose color B (1)

label.help: label used in material aov expresssions

references.help: list of geometries that geometry procedural can reference during procedural generate/update stages. For example, an instancer geometry procedural can instance primitives generated by the reference geometry procedural.

static.help: disable if the geometry will be updated between frames

side_type.help: set single sidedness of the mesh, will affect the visibility of the mesh based on normal direction

reverse_normals.help: enable to reverse the normals in the geometry

visible_in_camera.help: whether the geometry is visible to camera rays

visible_diffuse_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in diffuse reflection

visible_diffuse_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in diffuse transmission

visible_glossy_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in glossy reflection.

visible_glossy_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in glossy transmission (refraction).

visible_mirror_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in miror reflection.

visible_mirror_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in miror transmission (refraction).

visible_volume.help: whether the geometry is visible in indirect volume rays

ray_epsilon.help: When a secondary ray is fired, anything within this distance of the intersection point will be ignored.  Instead, it is considered part of the current intersection's geometry.  If zero, an automatically calculated epsilon will be used.

shadow_ray_epsilon.help: When a shadow ray is fired, anything within this distance of the intersection point will be ignored.  If this value is less than "ray_epsilon", then it has no additional effect.

shadow_receiver_label.help: Label used to associate Geometry objects into ShadowReceiverSets. Using this in combination with the ["shadow_exclusion_mappings"] attribute, shadows from specified geometries or their parts can be suppressed from casting shadows onto one or more specified sets.

shadow_exclusion_mappings.help:
A space-separated list of mappings of the form A:B where:
A is a comma-separated list of names of parts of this Geometry, or an asterisk to specify the whole geometry;
B is a comma-separated list of shadow receiver set labels established using the ["shadow_receiver_label"] attribute, or an asterisk to specify to all such sets in the scene.
For each of the listed mappings, shadows from the parts specified in A will be suppressed from casting onto any geometries in the ShadowReceiverSets specified in B.
**Note: no part name should appear more than once in the string, otherwise the behavior is undefined.**

contains_camera.help: Specifies whether the geometry contains the camera and should be used for IOR tracking. This should not be changed by the user -- they should instead attach the relevant geometry to the camera, which will then flag this geometry.

dicing_camera.help: Alternate camera that is used for adaptive tessellation.  This is useful if you want adaptive tessellation to behave consistently in a sequence, regardless of what the main camera is doing

use_local_motion_blur.help: Enables the local motion blur feature, which makes the geometry procedural responsible for handling all of the geometry's motion and allows for custom effects

use_local_camera_motion_blur.help: Enables experimental feature that also attempts to remove the camera blur in the local regions

num_u_tiles.help: number of checkerboard squares in the U direction

num_v_tiles.help: number of checkerboard squares in the V direction

texture_coordinates.help: switches between the model's uv coordinates or the input texture coordinates

input_texture_coordinates.help: bind a shader that outputs UV coordinates (such as a projection shader) here

mask.help: RenderOutput used to mask the output, revealing input1

invert_mask.help: Invert the value of the mask

input.help: the input values to be remapped

clamp.help: enables/disables clamping of the output values.  This useful prevent out-of-range values when expanding the input values.

clamp_min.help: the minimum value output by this map when 'clamp' is enabled

clamp_max.help: the maximum value output by this map when 'clamp' is enabled

mix.help: how much of the overall color correct to mix in

use_per_channel_contrast.help: enables separate RGB controls for contrast

contrast_r.help: negative values reduce the difference in luminance towards grey and positive values increase the difference in luminance for the red channel

contrast_g.help: negative values reduce the difference in luminance towards grey and positive values increase the difference in luminance for the green channel

contrast_b.help: negative values reduce the difference in luminance towards grey and positive values increase the difference in luminance for the blue channel

contrast.help: Negative values decrease contrast, while positive values increase it

saturation_shift.help: shifts the saturation of the input (-1, 1)

value_factor.help: multiplies the value of the input

use_xform.help: The filter can be bound to a light or lights position or when this toggle is set, can have its own transform

begin_distance.help: Where the ramp starts relative to the light or the ramp's independent transform

end_distance.help: Where the ramp ends relative to the light or the ramp's independent transform

colors.help: Vector of colors specified at different distances

distances.help: Distances between which colors are interpolated

interpolation_types.help: Interpolation types between the specified distances.  0: None 1: linear 2: exponential_up 3: exponential_down 4: smooth 5: catmull_rom

mode.help: Ramp: Radiates out from the center of the light or ramp location.  Directional: Linear starting at the location of the light or ramp location along negative z

wrap_mode.help: For directional filter mode where filter uses distance along -Z axis.  Extend: f(z) = f(0) for z > 0.  Mirror: f(z) = f(-z).

bound_padding.help: bound padding defines how much to extend the bounding box of the object. Keep this value as low as possible unless the geometry skips tessellation because control cage bounding box is out of camera frustum but the displacement stretch out of the original object bounding box (pre-displacement). Setting the bound padding too large will consume more memory and tessellation time.

operation.help: The method used for combining the displacements

light_filters.help: List of light filters to combine together

mode.help: How the light filters are combined

input_1.help: First normal map to combine; usually a base map

normal_map_1_dial.help: Amount of normal map 1 to blend in

input_2.help: Second normal map to combine; usually a base map

normal_map_2_dial.help: Amount of normal map 2 to blend in

mask.help: RenderOutput used to mask the output, revealing input1

invert_mask.help: Invert the value of the mask

kernel_size.help: Size of kernel in pixels. Size must be odd. If using a custom kernel, this attribute is ignored, and the size of the custom kernel is used instead.

custom_kernel.help: A list of kernel values for a custom filter. The number of values provided must be the square of an odd number (e.g. 3x3, 5x5, 7x7).

mask.help: RenderOutput used to mask the output, revealing input1

invert_mask.help: Invert the value of the mask

projector.help: If a projector is specified, it overrides the node_xform and projector_* attributes

projector_focal.help: Focal length of the lens when using perspective projection

projector_film_width_aperture.help: Size of the camera image plane

texture_map.help: Moonray map. Any Moonray map generator, checkerboard, noise, image map.  You may also add any of the map modifiers, color correct for example.  The default is an image map.

blur_near_value.help: Blur filter radius (in texture UV space) at the near distance

blur_mid_value.help: Blur filter radius (in texture UV space) at the mid distance

blur_far_value.help: Blur filter radius (in texture UV space) at the far distance

outside_projection.help: What happens outside the frustum of the projection camera.  Black (default), White, or Default (This uses the mode set on the Moonray map shader)

density.help: Controls how much of the cookie is added to the light

projector.help: If a projector is specified, it overrides the node_xform and projector_* attributes

projector_focal.help: Focal length of the lens when using perspective projection

projector_film_width_aperture.help: Size of the camera image plane

blur_near_value.help: Blur filter radius (in texture UV space) at the near distance

blur_mid_value.help: Blur filter radius (in texture UV space) at the mid distance

blur_far_value.help: Blur filter radius (in texture UV space) at the far distance

outside_projection.help: What happens outside the frustum of the projection camera.  Black (default), White, or Default (This uses the mode set on the Moonray map shader)

density.help: Controls how much of the cookie is added to the light

texture.help: File name of the texture applied to the light filter.  Any file format supported by OpenImageIO can be used.

gamma.help:
Per-channel gamma used in color-correcting the light filter's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  For input >  0, output = pow(input, gamma)
  For input <= 0, output = input

invert.help: Flips the value of the curvature.

power.help: Raises the value of the curvature to the given power.

scale.help: Multiplies the value of the curvature. Results are still clamped between 0 and 1.

mode.help: The composite mode outputs the composite of convex curvature and concave curvature as grayscale ((concave - convex) * 0.5) + 0.5. The all mode outputs the convex curvature in the red channel, concave curvature in the green channel, and composite of both curvatures in the blue channel.

label.help: label used in light aovs

bake_resolution_mode.help:
Method to specify grid resolution of baked density grid.  Choices are:
		"default": For shaders that are bound to vdb volumes, use vdb resolution.
						For shaders that are bounds to mesh geometries use 100 divisions
		"divisions": Specify number of divisions.
		"voxel size": Specify voxel size.

bake_divisions.help: Divide widest axis by this many divisions

bake_voxel_size.help: Size of voxel in world space

surface_opacity_threshold.help: Accumulated opacity that's considered the 'surface' for computing surface position and Z

indirect_volume.help: The volume to cutout / use for indirect illumination and occlusion.  Cutout behavior is invoked for primary rays but secondary/indirect rays are processed normally.

on.help: Whether the light is switched on.

mb.help: Whether motion-blur is active for this light. When set to true, the scene's illumination will correctly account for any blur() applied to the light's transformation matrix.

visible_in_camera.help: Whether the light is directly visible in the scene's active camera. When set to "use default" it reads from the value of SceneVariable lights_visible_in_camera.

color.help:
The light's RGB values.
These are combined multiplicatively with the intensity and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

intensity.help:
The light's intensity.
This is combined multiplicatively with the color and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

exposure.help:
The light's exposure value.
This value provides an alternative to the intensity value as a mechanism for controlling the light's overall brightness, and is inspired by the corresponding photographic term but is generalised to apply independently to each light. To calculate its effect, pow(2, exposure) is combined multiplicatively with the color and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

max_shadow_distance.help:
The distance from the light beyond which a light-receiving surface will no longer receive shadows cast from that light.
Note that the distance is thresholded for each occlusion ray cast for this light, it is possible for a receiving point to lie at an intermediate distance such that some parts of the light are closer than the threshold distance and other parts beyond it, in which case the point will appearto be in partial shadow.

min_shadow_distance.help:
The distance from the light before which a light-receiving surface will no longer receive shadows cast from that light.
Note that the distance is thresholded for each occlusion ray cast for this light, it is possible for a receiving point to lie at an intermediate distance such that some parts of the light are closer than the threshold distance and other parts beyond it, in which case the point will appearto be in partial shadow.

presence_shadows.help: Switch this attribute on for shadows cast from this light to correctly respect presence values. When off, surfaces with a material with presence less than 1.0 will cast opaque shadows from this light. This is an optimization - when the attribute is off, occlusion rays (fast) are used for testing for shadows. When it is on, regular rays (slower) are used, and the material's presence is evaluated to determine how much shadowing should occur. When set to "use default" it reads from the value of SceneVariable enable_presence_shadows.

ray_termination.help: Whether the light is used for ray termination color. Ray termination color is used for filling in falsely dark areas where ray paths have been terminated too early by the depth controls. Such a ray path immediately exits to any ray termination light(s) present in the light set being applied to the lobe, ignoring occlusion by scene geometry. Any light can either be a regular light or a ray termination light (but not both). Thus they can be freely assigned to light sets, which provides a mechanism for applying specific ray termination lights to specific materials, parts or objects. Ray termination color is only applied to non-hair transmission lobes.

texture_filter.help: The filtering mode to apply to the texture. Nearest neighbor is the cheapest filtering mode but produces a blocky result. Switch linear filtering on for a smoother result. Additionally, mip-mapping can be switched on with either nearest neighbor or linear filtering.

texture.help: File name of the texture applied to the light. If set to the empty string, no texture is applied. Any file format supported by OpenImageIO can be used. The texture is used in 2 ways - for looking up the texture value at the intersection point when a ray hits the light, and for building a lookup-table-based auxilliary data structure used for distributing light samples over the texture.

saturation.help:
Per-channel saturation used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = lerp(luminance(inputRGB), input, saturation).

contrast.help:
Per-channel contrast used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. The operation mimics Nuke's ColorCorrect node's contrast function:
  For input >  0, output = 0.18 * pow(inputCompnent/0.18, contrast).
  For input <= 0, output = 0.18 * input * pow(1/0.18, contrast).

gamma.help:
Per-channel gamma used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  For input >  0, output = pow(input, gamma)
  For input <= 0, output = input

gain.help:
Per-channel gain used in tandem with a per-channel offset for color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = input * gain + offset

offset.help:
Per-channel offset used in tandem with a per-channel gain for color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = input * gain + offset

temperature.help:
Color temperature using Nuke-style T/M/I settings (T = temperature, M = magenta/green, I = intensity). This is achieved as follows:
The 3-channel temperature is interpreted as the vector (T,M,I). The followiong scale values are then applied to the RGB components:
  outputR = inputR * (pow(2,I) + M/3 - T/2)
  outputG = inputG * (pow(2,I) - 2*M/3
  outputB = inputB * (pow(2,I) + M/3 + T/2)

texture_translation.help: Translation of the texture in (u,v)-space, in units of the texture size. For example, a translation of (0.25, 0.5) will translate the texture one-quarter of its width in the u-direction and one-half of its height in the v-direction.

texture_coverage.help: Texture scales in the u and v-directions.

texture_reps_u.help: Number of times texture repeats in u over the scaled texture space.

texture_reps_v.help: Number of times texture repeats in v over the scaled texture space.

texture_mirror_u.help: Whether to mirror the texture in the u-direction. If set to false, the texture is repeated in the u-direction.

texture_mirror_v.help: Whether to mirror the texture in the v-direction. If set to false, the texture is repeated in the v-direction.

texture_border_color.help: RGB value used when a texture lookup occurs outside the texture.

light_filters.help: Vector of LightFilters associated with the light.

label.help: Label used in light aov expressions.

visible_diffuse_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse reflection.

visible_diffuse_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse transmission.

visible_glossy_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy reflection.

visible_glossy_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy transmission (refraction).

visible_mirror_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in miror reflection.

visible_mirror_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in miror transmission (refraction).

normalized.help: When set to true, the size of the light can be changed without altering the amount of total energy cast into the scene. This is achieved via scaling the light's radiance by the reciprocal of its surface area. When set to false, the radiance is used as-is, regardless of surface area.

apply_scene_scale.help: Whether to apply scene scale variable when normalized.

height.help: The height of the cylinder as measured along its axis.

sidedness.help: When set to 0 (regular), light is emitted from the outward-facing surface of the cylinder. When set to 1 (reverse), light is emitted from the inward-facing surface of the cylinder. There is no mode for emitting from both surfaces at the same time, but two cylinder lights of opposing sidedness can be combined to achieve this effect.

clear_radius.help: Shadows less than this distance from the light are ignored. Setting this value to 0.0 or less effectively disables this feature.

clear_radius_falloff_distance.help: Distance over which the shadows fall off. Shadows are fully visible at a distance clear_radius + clear_radius_falloff_distance from the light, and fully invisble at a distance clear_radius from the light.

clear_radius_interpolation_type.help: Interpolation type to use for the clear radius shadow falloff.

input_normal_space.help: Specifies what space the input normal is in.  Usually this is tangent space for texture maps and render space for projections

primitive_attribute_name.help: the name of primitive attribute to displayed when attribute 'map type' is set to 'primitive attribute'

primitive_attribute_type.help: the type of primitive attribute to displayed when attribute 'map type' is set to 'primitive attribute'

near_start.help: distance from light to start of fade in

near_end.help: distance from light to end of fade in

far_start.help: distance from light to start of fade out

far_end.help: distance from light to end of fade out

output_mode.help:
Controls output: 
		    RGB - R = deformation along S, G = deformation along T, B = average deformation from ref space 
		    deformation_S - deformation along S 
		    deformation_T - deformation along T 
		    deformation_avg - average deformation from ref space

use_warning_color.help: If derivatives are missing or zero output the warning color erroring out

warning_color.help: Warning color to output when derivatives are missing or zero

prime_direction.help: which source is used for the prime direction

polarity.help: determines which directions are given color A and which are given color B. Switching this effectively swaps the colors

color_a.help: the color of the effect when the difference angle is greatest

color_b.help: the color of the effect when the difference angle is smallest

object.help: the object to use when 'prime direction' is set to 'axis of object' or 'look-at object'

object_axis.help: which axis to use when 'prime direction' is set to 'axis of object'

custom_direction.help: specifies a custom direction in world space as the prime direction

use_reference_space.help: use reference space position and normals

input_normal.help: specifies an alternate shading normal when bound. The binding multiplier is ignored

input_normal_dial.help: controls the amount of influence of the alternate normal

clamping_behavior.help: determines how surfaces facing opposite the prime direction are handled

bias.help: controls the rate at which the effect increases as the shading normal approaches the prime direction

smoothstep_start.help: the value at which the effect is considered 100% off

smoothstep_end.help: the value at which the effect is considered 100% on

falloff_type.help: determines how the effect falls off as the difference angle increases

mask.help: RenderOutput used to mask the output, revealing input1

invert_mask.help: Invert the value of the mask

on.help: Whether the light is switched on.

mb.help: Whether motion-blur is active for this light. When set to true, the scene's illumination will correctly account for any blur() applied to the light's transformation matrix.

visible_in_camera.help: Whether the light is directly visible in the scene's active camera. When set to "use default" it reads from the value of SceneVariable lights_visible_in_camera.

color.help:
The light's RGB values.
These are combined multiplicatively with the intensity and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

intensity.help:
The light's intensity.
This is combined multiplicatively with the color and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

exposure.help:
The light's exposure value.
This value provides an alternative to the intensity value as a mechanism for controlling the light's overall brightness, and is inspired by the corresponding photographic term but is generalised to apply independently to each light. To calculate its effect, pow(2, exposure) is combined multiplicatively with the color and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

max_shadow_distance.help:
The distance from the light beyond which a light-receiving surface will no longer receive shadows cast from that light.
Note that the distance is thresholded for each occlusion ray cast for this light, it is possible for a receiving point to lie at an intermediate distance such that some parts of the light are closer than the threshold distance and other parts beyond it, in which case the point will appearto be in partial shadow.

min_shadow_distance.help:
The distance from the light before which a light-receiving surface will no longer receive shadows cast from that light.
Note that the distance is thresholded for each occlusion ray cast for this light, it is possible for a receiving point to lie at an intermediate distance such that some parts of the light are closer than the threshold distance and other parts beyond it, in which case the point will appearto be in partial shadow.

presence_shadows.help: Switch this attribute on for shadows cast from this light to correctly respect presence values. When off, surfaces with a material with presence less than 1.0 will cast opaque shadows from this light. This is an optimization - when the attribute is off, occlusion rays (fast) are used for testing for shadows. When it is on, regular rays (slower) are used, and the material's presence is evaluated to determine how much shadowing should occur. When set to "use default" it reads from the value of SceneVariable enable_presence_shadows.

ray_termination.help: Whether the light is used for ray termination color. Ray termination color is used for filling in falsely dark areas where ray paths have been terminated too early by the depth controls. Such a ray path immediately exits to any ray termination light(s) present in the light set being applied to the lobe, ignoring occlusion by scene geometry. Any light can either be a regular light or a ray termination light (but not both). Thus they can be freely assigned to light sets, which provides a mechanism for applying specific ray termination lights to specific materials, parts or objects. Ray termination color is only applied to non-hair transmission lobes.

texture_filter.help: The filtering mode to apply to the texture. Nearest neighbor is the cheapest filtering mode but produces a blocky result. Switch linear filtering on for a smoother result. Additionally, mip-mapping can be switched on with either nearest neighbor or linear filtering.

texture.help: File name of the texture applied to the light. If set to the empty string, no texture is applied. Any file format supported by OpenImageIO can be used. The texture is used in 2 ways - for looking up the texture value at the intersection point when a ray hits the light, and for building a lookup-table-based auxilliary data structure used for distributing light samples over the texture.

saturation.help:
Per-channel saturation used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = lerp(luminance(inputRGB), input, saturation).

contrast.help:
Per-channel contrast used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. The operation mimics Nuke's ColorCorrect node's contrast function:
  For input >  0, output = 0.18 * pow(inputCompnent/0.18, contrast).
  For input <= 0, output = 0.18 * input * pow(1/0.18, contrast).

gamma.help:
Per-channel gamma used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  For input >  0, output = pow(input, gamma)
  For input <= 0, output = input

gain.help:
Per-channel gain used in tandem with a per-channel offset for color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = input * gain + offset

offset.help:
Per-channel offset used in tandem with a per-channel gain for color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = input * gain + offset

temperature.help:
Color temperature using Nuke-style T/M/I settings (T = temperature, M = magenta/green, I = intensity). This is achieved as follows:
The 3-channel temperature is interpreted as the vector (T,M,I). The followiong scale values are then applied to the RGB components:
  outputR = inputR * (pow(2,I) + M/3 - T/2)
  outputG = inputG * (pow(2,I) - 2*M/3
  outputB = inputB * (pow(2,I) + M/3 + T/2)

texture_translation.help: Translation of the texture in (u,v)-space, in units of the texture size. For example, a translation of (0.25, 0.5) will translate the texture one-quarter of its width in the u-direction and one-half of its height in the v-direction.

texture_coverage.help: Texture scales in the u and v-directions.

texture_reps_u.help: Number of times texture repeats in u over the scaled texture space.

texture_reps_v.help: Number of times texture repeats in v over the scaled texture space.

texture_mirror_u.help: Whether to mirror the texture in the u-direction. If set to false, the texture is repeated in the u-direction.

texture_mirror_v.help: Whether to mirror the texture in the v-direction. If set to false, the texture is repeated in the v-direction.

texture_border_color.help: RGB value used when a texture lookup occurs outside the texture.

light_filters.help: Vector of LightFilters associated with the light.

label.help: Label used in light aov expressions.

visible_diffuse_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse reflection.

visible_diffuse_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse transmission.

visible_glossy_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy reflection.

visible_glossy_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy transmission (refraction).

visible_mirror_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in miror reflection.

visible_mirror_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in miror transmission (refraction).

normalized.help: When set to true, the size of the light can be changed without altering the amount of total energy cast into the scene. This is achieved via scaling the light's radiance by the reciprocal of its surface area. When set to false, the radiance is used as-is, regardless of surface area.

apply_scene_scale.help: Whether to apply scene scale variable when normalized.

spread.help: The directionality of light emission. 1 is completely diffuse hemisphere. 0 is parallel to normal of light.

sidedness.help: When set to 0 (regular), light is emitted from the front-facing surface of the disk. When set to 1 (reverse), light is emitted from the back-facing surface of the disk. When set to 2 (2-sided), light is emitted from both surfaces of the disk.

clear_radius.help: Shadows less than this distance from the light are ignored. Setting this value to 0.0 or less effectively disables this feature.

clear_radius_falloff_distance.help: Distance over which the shadows fall off. Shadows are fully visible at a distance clear_radius + clear_radius_falloff_distance from the light, and fully invisble at a distance clear_radius from the light.

clear_radius_interpolation_type.help: Interpolation type to use for the clear radius shadow falloff.

on.help: Whether the light is switched on.

mb.help: Whether motion-blur is active for this light. When set to true, the scene's illumination will correctly account for any blur() applied to the light's transformation matrix.

visible_in_camera.help: Whether the light is directly visible in the scene's active camera. When set to "use default" it reads from the value of SceneVariable lights_visible_in_camera.

texture_reps_u.help: Number of times texture repeats in u over the scaled texture space.

texture_reps_v.help: Number of times texture repeats in v over the scaled texture space.

texture_mirror_v.help: Whether to mirror the texture in the v-direction. If set to false, the texture is repeated in the v-direction.

light_filters.help: Vector of LightFilters associated with the light.

label.help: Label used in light aov expressions.

visible_diffuse_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse reflection.

visible_diffuse_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse transmission.

visible_glossy_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy reflection.

visible_glossy_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy transmission (refraction).

visible_mirror_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in miror reflection.

visible_mirror_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in miror transmission (refraction).

normalized.help:
If set to true, a normalisation factor is applied to the light's radiance. The normalisation factor used is such that if a distant light of radiance (1,1,1) is directly overhead a Lambertian surface of colour (1,1,1), the  resulting outgoing radiance at the surface will be (1,1,1) regardless of the light's angular extent.
If set to false, the light's radiance is used as-is.

angular_extent.help: The angle in degrees subtended by the DistantLight's diameter.

noise_space.help: The space to calculate the noise in

use_input_vectors.help: when checked, use input_U and V. otherwise use geometry dPds/t

seed.help: the seed for the noise generation

frequency_U.help: controls noise frequency for U distortion

frequency_V.help: controls noise frequency for V distortion

input_normals.help: optional input to distort. if not connected, use geom normals

input.help: RenderOutput to which to apply depth of field

depth.help: The 'depth' RenderOutput to sample z-depth values from

use_camera_attributes.help: Read dof attributes from the active scene camera

mask.help: RenderOutput used to mask the output, revealing input1

invert_mask.help: Invert the value of the mask

mb_shutter_open.help: Frame at which the shutter opens, i.e., the beginning of the motion blur interval.

mb_shutter_close.help: Frame at which the shutter closes, i.e., the end of the motion blur interval.

mb_shutter_bias.help: Biases the motion blur samples toward one end of the shutter interval.

pixel_sample_map.help: Map indicating the number of pixel samples that should be used per pixel (in uniform sampling mode). This is a multiplier on the global pixel sample count specified in SceneVariables. If the provided map has incompatible dimensions, it will be resized.

medium_material.help: The material the camera is 'inside'. If no medium_geometry is specified, ALL rays will have this initial index of refraction applied.

medium_geometry.help: The geometry the camera is 'inside' to which you'd like the medium_material applied. (The use case for this is typically partially-submerged cameras)

extra_aovs.help: Bind this attribute to a 'ListMap' that contains references to ExtraAovMaps that specify additional outputs that can be assigned to a RenderOutput "light aov" result

label.help: label used in material and light aovs

priority.help: The material's place in an order of precedence for overlapping dielectrics. A value of 0 means the priority should be ignored. Materials with lower numbers (higher priority) "override" materials with higher numbers (lower priority).  To enable automatic removal of self-overlapping geometry, a non-zero priority must be set on the geometry's material.

record_reflected_cryptomatte.help: Indicates whether the next reflected surface should appear in the reflected cryptomatte layers

record_refracted_cryptomatte.help: Indicates whether the next refracted surface should appear in the refracted cryptomatte layers

mix.help: weight of adjustments applied to the material

emission_mode.help: how to handle emission input. masked uses mix input, unmasked is mix = 1

emission.help: emissive map to add to material's emission

adjust_specular.help: use specular adjustment attrs: specular_set, specular_set_blend, specular_mult

adjust_roughness.help: use roughness adjustment attrs: roughness_set, roughness_set_blend, roughness_mult, roughness_remap_{in/out}_{min/max}

adjust_color.help: use color adjustment attrs: color_hue_shift, color_saturation, color_gain

adjust_presence.help: use presence adjustment attrs: presence_set, presence_set_blend, presence_mult

casts_caustics.help: allows you to keep or set casts caustics attribute

thin_geometry.help: allows you to keep or set thin geometry attribute

disable_diffuse.help: when checked, turn off all diffuse from input

disable_clearcoat.help: when checked, turn off clearcoat from input

disable_specular.help: when checked, turn off all specular from input

extra_aovs.help: Bind this attribute to a 'ListMap' that contains references to ExtraAovMaps that specify additional outputs that can be assigned to a RenderOutput "light aov" result

label.help: label used in material and light aovs

priority.help: The material's place in an order of precedence for overlapping dielectrics. A value of 0 means the priority should be ignored. Materials with lower numbers (higher priority) "override" materials with higher numbers (lower priority).  To enable automatic removal of self-overlapping geometry, a non-zero priority must be set on the geometry's material.

record_reflected_cryptomatte.help: Indicates whether the next reflected surface should appear in the reflected cryptomatte layers

record_refracted_cryptomatte.help: Indicates whether the next refracted surface should appear in the refracted cryptomatte layers

fuzz_roughness.help: Lower values result in glancing angle highlights while higher values result in a broad, uniform coverage

use_absorbing_fuzz_fibers.help: Specify whether dark fuzz fibers absorb energy or transmit it to the layers below.

fuzz_normal.help: specifies an independent shading normal (normal map) for the fuzz lobe

fuzz_normal_dial.help: controls the amount of infuence of the alternate fuzz normal

clearcoat_model.help: sets the normal distribution function for clearcoat.  GGX is currently isotropic only

clearcoat_roughness.help: the roughness of the clearcoat lobe

clearcoat_thickness.help: the thickness of the virtual clearcoat layer. Values > 0 enable absorption

clearcoat_attenuation_color.help: the attenuation color of the clearcoat when 'cleacoat thickness' > 0

clearcoat_bending.help: (advanced, recommended ON) bends rays based on the clearcoat-refractive-index before evaluating the lobes under clearcoat

use_independent_clearcoat_normal.help: specifies whether the clearcoat lobe should use an independent normal

independent_clearcoat_normal.help: specifies an independent shading normal (normal map) for the clearcoat lobe

clearcoat_normal_dial.help: controls the amount of infuence of the alternate clearcoat normal

glitter_seed.help: The seed for the glitter random number generator

glitter_space.help: The space to calculate the worley noise in, defaults to reference space

glitter_density.help: controls the number of flakes per unit length; larger density packs more flakes into same space

glitter_layering_mode.help: layering mode for glitter on top of the under material. physical: conserves energy and glitter attenuates under material, additive: breaks energy conservation but glitter is never darker than the under material (eg. use case: snow)

glitter_style_A_frequency.help: 0 implies none of this style, 1 implies all the flakes will get this style

glitter_size_A.help: size of the flakes.  Apparent flake size may vary based on how much the flake spheres intersect the surface

glitter_roughness_A.help: specular roughness of individual flakes (0 makes flakes mirror-like)

glitter_color_A.help: base flake color (use physical metallic color values)

glitter_texture_A.help: filename that points to a texture .exr or .tx file (must be mip-mapped and tiled with maketx).

glitter_style_B_frequency.help: 0 implies none of this style, 1 implies all the flakes will get this style

glitter_size_B.help: size of the flakes.  Apparent flake size may vary based on how much the flake spheres intersect the surface

glitter_roughness_B.help: specular roughness of individual flakes (0 makes flakes mirror-like)

glitter_color_B.help: base flake color (use physical metallic color values)

glitter_texture_B.help: filename that points to a texture .exr or .tx file (must be mip-mapped and tiled with maketx).

glitter_color_hue_variation.help: introduce hue variation in flake color centered at the base flake color's hue on the hue wheel

glitter_color_saturation_variation.help: introduce saturation variation in flake color centered at the base flake color's saturation

glitter_color_value_variation.help: introduce value variation in flake color centered at the base flake color's value

glitter_jitter.help: Controls how much the flakes are randomly offset from a regular grid

glitter_compensate_reference_space_defor.help: (In ReferenceSpace) Compensates for stretch/compression/shear in glitter shapes resulting from animation etc

glitter_approximate_for_secondary_rays.help: use an approximation to shade glitter for non-mirror secondary rays

glitter_LOD_quality.help: controls quality of glitter at distances where individual flakes cannot be perceived; at lower values, approximation kicks in earlier

specular_lightset.help: lightset to use for specular lobes

specular_model.help: sets the normal distribution function for specular.  GGX is currently isotropic only

refractive_index.help: defines the Fresnel behavior, (affects reflection and refraction)

metallic_color.help: the overall reflection color, defines Fresnel behavior

metallic_edge_color.help: the reflection color at grazing angles, defines Fresnel behavior

roughness.help: the roughness of the surface (currently only affects reflection)

anisotropy.help: controls the shape of the primary reflection

iridescence.help: controls the strength of the iridescence effect

iridescence_apply_to.help: Apply iridescence to primary specular lobe or clearcoat/moisture lobe

iridescence_color_control.help: use hue interpolation: automatically cycles through hue wheel, use ramp: user specified color ramp

iridescence_primary_color.help: First color to interpolate from around the hue wheel

iridescence_secondary_color.help: Second color to interpolate to around the hue wheel

iridescence_flip_hue_direction.help: flip interpolation around the hue wheel to counter-clockwise direction

iridescence_ramp_interpolation_mode.help: RGB: lerp in RGB space which matches UI preview but can lose saturation, HSV: lerp in HSV space which preserves saturation

iridescence_colors.help: List of colors on the ramp

iridescence_interpolations.help:
None: 0 | Linear: 1 | Exponential Up: 2 | Exponential Down: 3 |
			Smooth: 4 | Catmull Rom: 5 | Monotone Cubic: 6

iridescence_thickness.help: Controls how much the color spectrum is repeated

iridescence_exponent.help: Tightens or broadens the distribution of colors

iridescence_at_0_incidence.help: Iridescence effect at 0 degree viewing angle

iridescence_at_90_incidence.help: Iridescence effect at 90 degree viewing angle

diffuse_lightset.help: lightset to use for diffuse lobes

albedo.help: the overall surface color as seen from a distance (ie. diffuse color)

diffuse_roughness.help: Roughness of the diffuse shading.  If the value is zero a Lambertian model is used.  If it's above zero the Oren Nayar model is used.   Not compatible with subsurface scattering.

bssrdf.help: 0 for NormalizedDiffuse, 1 for Dipole, 2 for random walk

scattering_radius.help: the distance the light scatters beneath the surface. When 0 surface diffuse (lambertian or toon) is used

crease_attenuation.help: This mitigates the over-brightening around creases which random walk SSS can cause.

enable_sss_input_normal.help: enables sampling the normal map for sss samples. More accurate but potentially expensive

resolve_self_intersections.help: tries to resolve self-intersecting geometry automatically by only evaluating 'exiting' intersections for subsurface evaluations

diffuse_transmission.help: multiplier on the amount of light that is transmitted through the surface.

diffuse_transmission_color.help: the color/amount of light that is transmitted through the surface.

diffuse_transmission_blending_behavior.help: Controls how diffuse transmission color attenuates diffuse reflection

transmission.help: enables/disables refractive solid model (binary 0|1 for plausibility)

transmission_color.help: the desired color of transmitted light

use_independent_transmission_refractive_.help: use a separate IOR for transmission

independent_transmission_refractive_inde.help: defines a separate IOR for the bending of light with transmission

use_independent_transmission_roughness.help: use a separate roughness for transmission

dispersion_abbe_number.help: The amount of dispersion/chromatic-aberration via refractions. Lower this number to increase the effect. A value of 0 turns off dispersion. Around [25-80] makes sense for realistic glass. Lower values may look better on gemstones.

emission.help: the energy emitted from this material

input_normal.help: specifies an alternate shading normal in the tangent frame (normal map)

input_normal_dial.help: controls the amount of influence of the alternate normal

normal_AA_strategy.help: Normal map anti-aliasing strategy to use - 'none' uses regular mip-mapping, 'toksvig' increases specular roughness corresponding to the geometric details filtered out because of mip-mapping.

normal_AA_dial.help: Controls the amount roughness compensation from the normal map AA strategy.

presence.help: controls the visibility of this object. Useful for fading an object in/out, or to specify a cut-out mask on thin single-sided geometry (eg. a complex leaf texture on a simple card).

thin_geometry.help: enables proper shading of infinitely thin geometry such as paper or leaves.

casts_caustics.help: allows continuation of caustic light paths.

sss_trace_set.help: Set of geometries that contribute neighboring subsurface points. By default, only the geometry associated with this material contributes to subsurface. If you want adjacent geometry with different material to contribute as well, specify all those parts here.

extra_aovs.help: Bind this attribute to a 'ListMap' that contains references to ExtraAovMaps that specify additional outputs that can be assigned to a RenderOutput "light aov" result

label.help: label used in material and light aovs

priority.help: The material's place in an order of precedence for overlapping dielectrics. A value of 0 means the priority should be ignored. Materials with lower numbers (higher priority) "override" materials with higher numbers (lower priority).  To enable automatic removal of self-overlapping geometry, a non-zero priority must be set on the geometry's material.

record_reflected_cryptomatte.help: Indicates whether the next reflected surface should appear in the reflected cryptomatte layers

record_refracted_cryptomatte.help: Indicates whether the next refracted surface should appear in the refracted cryptomatte layers

mix.help: how much of the overall color correct to mix in

hue_shift.help: shifts the hue of the input (spectrum range is 0-1)

saturation.help: desaturates the input below 1.0 and adds saturation above 1.0

gain.help: multiplies the input channels by the specified value

TMI.help: T = Temperature where positive values push towards blue and negative towards red.   M = Magenta where positive values push towards magenta and negative towards green.  I = Intensity where negative values remove and positive values add energy

extra_aovs.help: Bind this attribute to a 'ListMap' that contains references to ExtraAovMaps that specify additional outputs that can be assigned to a RenderOutput "light aov" result

fuzz_roughness.help: Lower values result in glancing angle highlights while higher values result in a broad, uniform coverage

use_absorbing_fuzz_fibers.help: Specify whether dark fuzz fibers absorb energy or transmit it to the layers below.

fuzz_normal.help: specifies an independent shading normal (normal map) for the fuzz lobe

fuzz_normal_dial.help: controls the amount of infuence of the alternate fuzz normal

glitter_seed.help: The seed for the glitter random number generator

glitter_space.help: The space to calculate the worley noise in, defaults to reference space

glitter_density.help: controls the number of flakes per unit length; larger density packs more flakes into same space

glitter_layering_mode.help: layering mode for glitter on top of the under material. physical: conserves energy and glitter attenuates under material, additive: breaks energy conservation but glitter is never darker than the under material (eg. use case: snow)

glitter_style_A_frequency.help: 0 implies none of this style, 1 implies all the flakes will get this style

glitter_size_A.help: size of the flakes.  Apparent flake size may vary based on how much the flake spheres intersect the surface

glitter_roughness_A.help: specular roughness of individual flakes (0 makes flakes mirror-like)

glitter_color_A.help: base flake color (use physical metallic color values)

glitter_texture_A.help: filename that points to a texture .exr or .tx file (must be mip-mapped and tiled with maketx).

glitter_style_B_frequency.help: 0 implies none of this style, 1 implies all the flakes will get this style

glitter_size_B.help: size of the flakes.  Apparent flake size may vary based on how much the flake spheres intersect the surface

glitter_roughness_B.help: specular roughness of individual flakes (0 makes flakes mirror-like)

glitter_color_B.help: base flake color (use physical metallic color values)

glitter_texture_B.help: filename that points to a texture .exr or .tx file (must be mip-mapped and tiled with maketx).

glitter_color_hue_variation.help: introduce hue variation in flake color centered at the base flake color's hue on the hue wheel

glitter_color_saturation_variation.help: introduce saturation variation in flake color centered at the base flake color's saturation

glitter_color_value_variation.help: introduce value variation in flake color centered at the base flake color's value

glitter_jitter.help: Controls how much the flakes are randomly offset from a regular grid

glitter_compensate_reference_space_defor.help: (In ReferenceSpace) Compensates for stretch/compression/shear in glitter shapes resulting from animation etc

glitter_approximate_for_secondary_rays.help: use an approximation to shade glitter for non-mirror secondary rays

glitter_LOD_quality.help: controls quality of glitter at distances where individual flakes cannot be perceived; at lower values, approximation kicks in earlier

warp_color.help: Color of the fabric specular highlights. Or if 'use independent weft attributes' is 'true,' just the color of the fabric specular highlights of the warp threads.

warp_roughness.help: Roughness of the fabric specular highlights. Or if 'use independent weft attributes' is 'true,' just the roughness of the fabric specular highlights of the warp threads.

use_independent_weft_attributes.help: Use a separate set of attributes for the 'weft' thread specular response.

weft_color.help: Color of the fabric specular highlights from the weft threads.

weft_roughness.help: Roughness of the fabric specular highlights from the weft threads.

use_UVs_for_thread_direction.help: Use UV texture coordinates to compute thread direction (along dPds). Switch OFF for seamless camera aligned thread direction.  The warp_thread_direction parameter rotates this direction in tangent space.

fabric_diffuse_scattering.help: Amount of diffuse to mix in fabric. A value of 1 means fully diffuse fabric.

albedo.help: the overall surface color as seen from a distance (ie. diffuse color)

diffuse_roughness.help: Roughness of the diffuse shading.  If the value is zero a Lambertian model is used.  If it's above zero the Oren Nayar model is used.   Not compatible with subsurface scattering.

diffuse_transmission.help: multiplier on the amount of light that is transmitted through the surface.

diffuse_transmission_color.help: the color/amount of light that is transmitted through the surface.

diffuse_transmission_blending_behavior.help: Controls how diffuse transmission color attenuates diffuse reflection

warp_thread_direction.help: Direction of the major/warp thread for the fabric. The fabric highlights will be relative to this direction.

warp_thread_coverage.help: When set to 1, creates specular highlights only relative to the warp thread direction. When less than one, starts acting like a 'woven' fabric with specular contributions added in by a corresponding 'weft' thread direction, perpendicular to the 'warp' thread direction.

warp_thread_elevation.help: This attribute can be used to 'elevate/rotate' the threads from their default direction. When laying flat on the surface (thread_direction.z=0), a value around 45 works well for 'silky' looks. Range - -90, +90. When threads are upright (thread_direction.z=1), this attribute can be used to 'dishevel' the threads and create a 'velvety' look (see wiki).

input_normal_dial.help: controls the amount of influence of the alternate normal

presence.help: controls the visibility of this object. Useful for fading an object in/out, or to specify a cut-out mask on thin single-sided geometry (eg. a complex leaf texture on a simple card).

thin_geometry.help: enables proper shading of infinitely thin geometry such as paper or leaves.

casts_caustics.help: allows continuation of caustic light paths.

glitter_texture_A.help: filename that points to a texture .exr or .tx file (must be mip-mapped and tiled with maketx).

glitter_color_saturation_variation.help: introduce saturation variation in flake color centered at the base flake color's saturation

glitter_color_value_variation.help: introduce value variation in flake color centered at the base flake color's value

glitter_compensate_reference_space_defor.help: (In ReferenceSpace) Compensates for stretch/compression/shear in glitter shapes resulting from animation etc

glitter_LOD_quality.help: controls quality of glitter at distances where individual flakes cannot be perceived; at lower values, approximation kicks in earlier

diffuse_transmission.help: multiplier on the amount of light that is transmitted through the surface.

diffuse_transmission_color.help: the color/amount of light that is transmitted through the surface.

diffuse_transmission_blending_behavior.help: Controls how diffuse transmission color attenuates diffuse reflection

on.help: Whether the light is switched on.

mb.help: Whether motion-blur is active for this light. When set to true, the scene's illumination will correctly account for any blur() applied to the light's transformation matrix.

visible_in_camera.help: Whether the light is directly visible in the scene's active camera. When set to "use default" it reads from the value of SceneVariable lights_visible_in_camera.

color.help:
The light's RGB values.
These are combined multiplicatively with the intensity and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

intensity.help:
The light's intensity.
This is combined multiplicatively with the color and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

exposure.help:
The light's exposure value.
This value provides an alternative to the intensity value as a mechanism for controlling the light's overall brightness, and is inspired by the corresponding photographic term but is generalised to apply independently to each light. To calculate its effect, pow(2, exposure) is combined multiplicatively with the color and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

max_shadow_distance.help:
The distance from the light beyond which a light-receiving surface will no longer receive shadows cast from that light.
Note that the distance is thresholded for each occlusion ray cast for this light, it is possible for a receiving point to lie at an intermediate distance such that some parts of the light are closer than the threshold distance and other parts beyond it, in which case the point will appearto be in partial shadow.

min_shadow_distance.help:
The distance from the light before which a light-receiving surface will no longer receive shadows cast from that light.
Note that the distance is thresholded for each occlusion ray cast for this light, it is possible for a receiving point to lie at an intermediate distance such that some parts of the light are closer than the threshold distance and other parts beyond it, in which case the point will appearto be in partial shadow.

presence_shadows.help: Switch this attribute on for shadows cast from this light to correctly respect presence values. When off, surfaces with a material with presence less than 1.0 will cast opaque shadows from this light. This is an optimization - when the attribute is off, occlusion rays (fast) are used for testing for shadows. When it is on, regular rays (slower) are used, and the material's presence is evaluated to determine how much shadowing should occur. When set to "use default" it reads from the value of SceneVariable enable_presence_shadows.

ray_termination.help: Whether the light is used for ray termination color. Ray termination color is used for filling in falsely dark areas where ray paths have been terminated too early by the depth controls. Such a ray path immediately exits to any ray termination light(s) present in the light set being applied to the lobe, ignoring occlusion by scene geometry. Any light can either be a regular light or a ray termination light (but not both). Thus they can be freely assigned to light sets, which provides a mechanism for applying specific ray termination lights to specific materials, parts or objects. Ray termination color is only applied to non-hair transmission lobes.

texture_filter.help: The filtering mode to apply to the texture. Nearest neighbor is the cheapest filtering mode but produces a blocky result. Switch linear filtering on for a smoother result. Additionally, mip-mapping can be switched on with either nearest neighbor or linear filtering.

texture.help: File name of the texture applied to the light. If set to the empty string, no texture is applied. Any file format supported by OpenImageIO can be used. The texture is used in 2 ways - for looking up the texture value at the intersection point when a ray hits the light, and for building a lookup-table-based auxilliary data structure used for distributing light samples over the texture.

saturation.help:
Per-channel saturation used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = lerp(luminance(inputRGB), input, saturation).

contrast.help:
Per-channel contrast used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. The operation mimics Nuke's ColorCorrect node's contrast function:
  For input >  0, output = 0.18 * pow(inputCompnent/0.18, contrast).
  For input <= 0, output = 0.18 * input * pow(1/0.18, contrast).

gamma.help:
Per-channel gamma used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  For input >  0, output = pow(input, gamma)
  For input <= 0, output = input

gain.help:
Per-channel gain used in tandem with a per-channel offset for color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = input * gain + offset

offset.help:
Per-channel offset used in tandem with a per-channel gain for color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = input * gain + offset

temperature.help:
Color temperature using Nuke-style T/M/I settings (T = temperature, M = magenta/green, I = intensity). This is achieved as follows:
The 3-channel temperature is interpreted as the vector (T,M,I). The followiong scale values are then applied to the RGB components:
  outputR = inputR * (pow(2,I) + M/3 - T/2)
  outputG = inputG * (pow(2,I) - 2*M/3
  outputB = inputB * (pow(2,I) + M/3 + T/2)

texture_translation.help: Translation of the texture in (u,v)-space, in units of the texture size. For example, a translation of (0.25, 0.5) will translate the texture one-quarter of its width in the u-direction and one-half of its height in the v-direction.

texture_coverage.help: Texture scales in the u and v-directions.

texture_reps_u.help: Number of times texture repeats in u over the scaled texture space.

texture_reps_v.help: Number of times texture repeats in v over the scaled texture space.

texture_mirror_u.help: Whether to mirror the texture in the u-direction. If set to false, the texture is repeated in the u-direction.

texture_mirror_v.help: Whether to mirror the texture in the v-direction. If set to false, the texture is repeated in the v-direction.

texture_border_color.help: RGB value used when a texture lookup occurs outside the texture.

light_filters.help: Vector of LightFilters associated with the light.

visible_diffuse_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse reflection.

visible_diffuse_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse transmission.

visible_glossy_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy reflection.

visible_glossy_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy transmission (refraction).

visible_mirror_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in miror reflection.

visible_mirror_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in miror transmission (refraction).

sample_upper_hemisphere_only.help: Set this to true if you want the EnvLight to illuminate from only the "upper" hemisphere, defined as the hemisphere centered around the light's positive local z-axis direction.

The purpose of this setting is to efficiently capture information from all rays that leave a surface that could potentially intersect and trigger aov evaluation on other surfaces.

mb_shutter_open.help: Frame at which the shutter opens, i.e., the beginning of the motion blur interval.

mb_shutter_close.help: Frame at which the shutter closes, i.e., the end of the motion blur interval.

mb_shutter_bias.help: Biases the motion blur samples toward one end of the shutter interval.

pixel_sample_map.help: Map indicating the number of pixel samples that should be used per pixel (in uniform sampling mode). This is a multiplier on the global pixel sample count specified in SceneVariables. If the provided map has incompatible dimensions, it will be resized.

medium_material.help: The material the camera is 'inside'. If no medium_geometry is specified, ALL rays will have this initial index of refraction applied.

medium_geometry.help: The geometry the camera is 'inside' to which you'd like the medium_material applied. (The use case for this is typically partially-submerged cameras)

format.help: The format for fitting the circle to the image - see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fisheye_lens

zoom.help: Scaling factor applied on top of the format scale

fov.help: Field of view measured in degrees

end.help: End position in the chosen space

falloff_bias.help: Compresses the blending towards the start or end color
