use_independent_front_and_back_hair_colo.houdini_label: Use Independent Front And Back Hair Color
use_independent_front_and_back_hair_colo.help: (advanced) use a separate hair color for front and back

front_hair_color.help: (advanced only) hair color used for front-lit hair (backward reflectance)

back_hair_color.help: (advanced only) hair color used for back-lit hair (transmission/forward reflectance)

subsurface_blend.help: 0 is fully hair diffuse, 1 is fully SSS. No effect if scattering radius is 0.

bssrdf.help: 0 for NormalizedDiffuse, 1 for Dipole. Random walk unsupported for hair.

scattering_radius.help: the distance the light scatters beneath the surface.  When 0 surface diffuse is used

sss_trace_set.help: Set of geometries that contribute neighboring subsurface points. By default, only the geometry associated with this material contributes to subsurface. If you want adjacent geometry with different material to contribute as well, specify all those parts here.

enable_sss_input_normal.help: enables sampling the normal map for sss samples. More accurate but potentially expensive

input_normal.help: specifies an alternate shading normal (only for SSS lobe)

input_normal_dial.help: controls influence of input normal versus hair normal for SSS

presence.help: controls the visibility of this object. Useful for fading an object in/out, or to specify a cut-out mask on thin single-sided geometry (eg. a complex leaf texture on a simple card).

diffuse_lightset.help: lightset to use for diffuse lobes

specular_lightset.help: lightset to use for specular lobes

emission.help: the energy emitted from this material

input.help: RenderOutput to be represented in halftone

size.help: The size in pixels of the halftone dots

filter_width.help: The width in pixels of the antialiasing

invert.help: Whether the dots should be black (normal) or white/color (inverted)

grayscale.help: Ignore color information, render as grayscale

mask.help: RenderOutput used to mask the output, revealing input1

invert_mask.help: Invert the value of the mask

input.help: RenderOutput to use in the ImageDisplayFilter

image_path.help: File path to the .exr we want to fit to the image plane

display_type.help: Method used to fit the input image to the image plane.

mask.help: RenderOutput used to mask the output, revealing input1

invert_mask.help: Invert the value of the mask

wrap_around.help: If true, the image will repeat itself at it's boundries.   If false, the image will be clamped at it's boundries.

texture.help: Filename that points to a texture .exr or .tx file (must be mip-mapped and tiled with maketx). If filename contains '<UDIM>', udim index substitution is performed on filename.  In the UDIM case, 'scale', 'offset', and 'wrap around' are ignored.

use_default_color_when_missing.help: Uses the 'default color' for missing udims and does not report error

default_color.help: Default color to be used for missing udims when 'use default color when missing' is enabled

texture_coordinates.help: Selects the source of the coordinates used to map the texture.  For meshes, the 'texture' setting will read a Vec2f attribute named 'surface_st' if it exists, otherwise it will use barycentric coordinates.   For points and curves, the 'texture' setting will read a Vec2f attribute named 'uv' if it exists.   If the attribute doesn't exist, parametric st coordinates are used for curves and a constant value of (1.0, 1.0) is used for points.  The 'input texture coordinates' setting will evaluate a binding on the 'input_texture_coordinates' parameter and use the resulting red and green channels as the texture coordinates.   The 'hair surface' setting will try to read an explicit attribute named 'surface_st'.   The 'hair closest surface' setting will try to read an explicit attribute named 'closest_surface_uv'.

alpha_only.help: If true, the alpha channel of the texture will be placed in the rgb channels.  If the texture has no alpha channel, 1.0 is used, and the resulting texture lookup is then always white.

input_texture_coordinates.help: If the 'texture_coordinates' parameter is set to 'input texture coordinates' then this parameter is evaluated and the resulting red and green channels are used for the u and v coordinates respectively.

mip_bias.help: Amount to offset derivatives which controls mipmap selection.   This can be used to provide a cheap, albiet low quality, blur.  Note: Setting this value to -1.0 effectively disables mip mapping and the highest resolution will be used.

gamma.help: If this is set to 'on' or 'auto' and the 'texture' file is 8-bit, then a power of 2.2 will be applied to the RGB channels of the image.

offset.help: A constant offset applied to the texture coordinates.

scale.help: A constant scale applied to the texture coordinates.

rotation_angle.help: A constant rotation in degrees applied to the texture coordinates.

rotation_center.help: UV coordinate around which to rotate

saturation.help: Applies saturation per channel with 0.0 being grayscale and 1.0 being full saturation

contrast.help: Applies contrast per channel in the style of Nuke where the pivot is at 0.18

TMI.help: T = Temperature where positive values push towards blue and negative towards red.   M = Magenta where positive values push towards magenta and negative towards green.  I = Intensity where negative values remove and positive values add energy

tangent_space_normal_texture.help: Filename that points to a tangent space normal texture .exr or .tx file (must be mip-mapped and tiled with maketx).   If 'udim_files' is empty and a 'UDIM' token is present in the filename, then 'UDIM' will be replaced with the udim number (i.e. 1001, 1002, etc.)

use_default_value_when_missing.help: Uses the 'default_value' for missing udims and does not report error

default_value.help: Default value to be used for missing udims when 'use_default_value_when_missing' is enabled

wrap_around.help: Controls whether to repeat (true) or clamp (false) the texture

normal_encoding.help: Most normal maps are encoded [0,1]. Only certain rare floating point normal maps are encoded [-1,1]

texture_coordinates.help: Selects which texture coordinates to use.   'texture' uses the surface UVs.   'input texture coordinates' uses the map bound to 'input_texture_coordinates'.

input_texture_coordinates.help: When 'texture_coordinates' is set to 'input texture coordinates', UVs are generated by evaluating the map bound to this attribute

offset.help: Translates the UV coordinates in texture space.  The order of operations is rotation, scale, and then offset.

scale.help: Scales the UV coordinates in texture space.  The order of operations is rotation, scale, and then offset.

rotation_angle.help: Rotation of the UV coordinates in texture space in degrees.  The order of operations is rotation, scale, and then offset.

rotation_center.help: The UV coordinate around which to rotate.  The order of operations is rotation, scale, and then offset.

intensity.help: Multiply the light radiance by this intensity value

exposure.help: Multiply the light radiance by exposure = pow(2, exposure)

color.help: Multiply the light radiance by this RGB color value

light_path_selection.help: Controls which light paths the filter is applied to.

mode.help: Use feature_width for LOD based on average, world-space feature-width visible in a pixel, correctly changing with resolution. Use camera_distance for LOD based on distance from render cam.

start.help: feature_width/camera_distance at which to start blending near_value->far_value

stop.help: feature_width/camera_distance at which to stop blending near_value->far_value

near_value.help: value output when feature_width/camera_distance is less than or equal to start

far_value.help: value output when feature_width/camera_distance is more than or equal to stop

lightfilters.help: List of light filters that belong to this LightFilterSet

lights.help: List of lights that belong to this LightSet

on.help: Whether the light is switched on.

mb.help: Whether motion-blur is active for this light. When set to true, the scene's illumination will correctly account for any blur() applied to the light's transformation matrix.

visible_in_camera.help: Whether the light is directly visible in the scene's active camera. When set to "use default" it reads from the value of SceneVariable lights_visible_in_camera.

color.help:
The light's RGB values.
These are combined multiplicatively with the intensity and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

intensity.help:
The light's intensity.
This is combined multiplicatively with the color and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

exposure.help:
The light's exposure value.
This value provides an alternative to the intensity value as a mechanism for controlling the light's overall brightness, and is inspired by the corresponding photographic term but is generalised to apply independently to each light. To calculate its effect, pow(2, exposure) is combined multiplicatively with the color and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

max_shadow_distance.help:
The distance from the light beyond which a light-receiving surface will no longer receive shadows cast from that light.
Note that the distance is thresholded for each occlusion ray cast for this light, it is possible for a receiving point to lie at an intermediate distance such that some parts of the light are closer than the threshold distance and other parts beyond it, in which case the point will appearto be in partial shadow.

min_shadow_distance.help:
The distance from the light before which a light-receiving surface will no longer receive shadows cast from that light.
Note that the distance is thresholded for each occlusion ray cast for this light, it is possible for a receiving point to lie at an intermediate distance such that some parts of the light are closer than the threshold distance and other parts beyond it, in which case the point will appearto be in partial shadow.

presence_shadows.help: Switch this attribute on for shadows cast from this light to correctly respect presence values. When off, surfaces with a material with presence less than 1.0 will cast opaque shadows from this light. This is an optimization - when the attribute is off, occlusion rays (fast) are used for testing for shadows. When it is on, regular rays (slower) are used, and the material's presence is evaluated to determine how much shadowing should occur. When set to "use default" it reads from the value of SceneVariable enable_presence_shadows.

ray_termination.help: Whether the light is used for ray termination color. Ray termination color is used for filling in falsely dark areas where ray paths have been terminated too early by the depth controls. Such a ray path immediately exits to any ray termination light(s) present in the light set being applied to the lobe, ignoring occlusion by scene geometry. Any light can either be a regular light or a ray termination light (but not both). Thus they can be freely assigned to light sets, which provides a mechanism for applying specific ray termination lights to specific materials, parts or objects. Ray termination color is only applied to non-hair transmission lobes.

texture_filter.help: The filtering mode to apply to the texture. Nearest neighbor is the cheapest filtering mode but produces a blocky result. Switch linear filtering on for a smoother result. Additionally, mip-mapping can be switched on with either nearest neighbor or linear filtering.

texture.help: File name of the texture applied to the light. If set to the empty string, no texture is applied. Any file format supported by OpenImageIO can be used. The texture is used in 2 ways - for looking up the texture value at the intersection point when a ray hits the light, and for building a lookup-table-based auxilliary data structure used for distributing light samples over the texture.

saturation.help:
Per-channel saturation used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = lerp(luminance(inputRGB), input, saturation).

contrast.help:
Per-channel contrast used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. The operation mimics Nuke's ColorCorrect node's contrast function:
  For input >  0, output = 0.18 * pow(inputCompnent/0.18, contrast).
  For input <= 0, output = 0.18 * input * pow(1/0.18, contrast).

gamma.help:
Per-channel gamma used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  For input >  0, output = pow(input, gamma)
  For input <= 0, output = input

gain.help:
Per-channel gain used in tandem with a per-channel offset for color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = input * gain + offset

offset.help:
Per-channel offset used in tandem with a per-channel gain for color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = input * gain + offset

temperature.help:
Color temperature using Nuke-style T/M/I settings (T = temperature, M = magenta/green, I = intensity). This is achieved as follows:
The 3-channel temperature is interpreted as the vector (T,M,I). The followiong scale values are then applied to the RGB components:
  outputR = inputR * (pow(2,I) + M/3 - T/2)
  outputG = inputG * (pow(2,I) - 2*M/3
  outputB = inputB * (pow(2,I) + M/3 + T/2)

texture_translation.help: Translation of the texture in (u,v)-space, in units of the texture size. For example, a translation of (0.25, 0.5) will translate the texture one-quarter of its width in the u-direction and one-half of its height in the v-direction.

texture_coverage.help: Texture scales in the u and v-directions.

texture_reps_u.help: Number of times texture repeats in u over the scaled texture space.

texture_reps_v.help: Number of times texture repeats in v over the scaled texture space.

texture_mirror_u.help: Whether to mirror the texture in the u-direction. If set to false, the texture is repeated in the u-direction.

texture_mirror_v.help: Whether to mirror the texture in the v-direction. If set to false, the texture is repeated in the v-direction.

texture_border_color.help: RGB value used when a texture lookup occurs outside the texture.

light_filters.help: Vector of LightFilters associated with the light.

label.help: Label used in light aov expressions.

visible_diffuse_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse reflection.

visible_diffuse_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse transmission.

visible_glossy_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy reflection.

visible_glossy_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy transmission (refraction).

visible_mirror_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in miror reflection.

visible_mirror_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in miror transmission (refraction).

normalized.help: When set to true, the size of the light can be changed without altering the amount of total energy cast into the scene. This is achieved via scaling the light's radiance by the reciprocal of its surface area. When set to false, the radiance is used as-is, regardless of surface area.

apply_scene_scale.help: Whether to apply scene scale variable when normalized.

geometry.help: The SceneObject holding the mesh data to use as the MeshLight's surface.

parts.help: The parts list to use. If empty, all parts are used.

map_shader.help: A Map shader to sample for radiance values.

clear_radius.help: Shadows less than this distance from the light are ignored. Setting this value to 0.0 or less effectively disables this feature.

clear_radius_falloff_distance.help: Distance over which the shadows fall off. Shadows are fully visible at a distance clear_radius + clear_radius_falloff_distance from the light, and fully invisble at a distance clear_radius from the light.

clear_radius_interpolation_type.help: Interpolation type to use for the clear radius shadow falloff.

type.help:
Allowed types for exr headers:
			* box2i
			* box2f
			* chromaticities
			* double
			* float
			* int
			* m33f
			* m44f
			* string
			* v2i
			* v2f
			* v3i
			* v3f

mode.help: Specifies which color channels including combination channels (Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, and White) to convert to float.

tolerance.help: Low tolerance values will tend to produce harsher edges near overlapping colors, while high tolerance values may result in cross-color bleeding.

seed.help: The seed for the random number generator

frequency_multiplier.help: Scalar multiplier for the frequency vector

max_level.help: Number of octaves of noise to add together for the final result

lacunarity.help: Multiplier on the noise frequency per level

persistence.help: Multiplier on the noise amplitude per level

distortion.help: Warp input coordinate space with single noise level before looking up noise

distortion_noise_type.help: Type of noise to use for distortion.

noise_type.help: Type of noise to use. Simplex grid activates Flow Noise Angle and Advection

flow_angle.help: Angle of rotation for flow noise

flow_advection_rate.help: Rate of advection for flow noise

use_4D_noise.help: If on, 4-dimensional noise is used instead of 3-dimensional

time.help: If use 4D noise is on, this is the value for the 4th dimension

use_smoothstep.help: Put the noise value through a smoothstep function defined by min/max

smoothstep.help: min/max values between which the smoothstep will interpolate

space.help: The space to calculate the noise in

object_space.help: Directly connect object to use that object's space

camera.help: camera used to define camera and screen space

rotation.help: Rotates the noise in space based on the specified rotation order

scale.help: Vector to scale the noise non-proportionally

rotation_order.help: Order in which to apply the euler rotations

transformation_order.help: Order in which to apply the translation, rotation, and frequency

seed.help: The seed for the random number generator

frequency.help: Scalar multiplier for the frequency vector

jitter.help: Controls the distortion of the cells

color_B.help: The interpolated color value at distance equals one

distance_method.help: Metric for calculating distance to feature points which controls the shape of the falloff when output mode is distance

minkowski_number.help: Exponent on distances when distance method is set to Minkowski

bound_padding.help: bound padding defines how much to extend the bounding box of the object. Keep this value as low as possible unless the geometry skips tessellation because control cage bounding box is out of camera frustum but the displacement stretch out of the original object bounding box (pre-displacement). Setting the bound padding too large will consume more memory and tessellation time.

height.help: This parameter is used as the initial displacement before the zero_value and height_multiplier are applied.

zero_value.help: This value is added to the displacement before applying the height_multiplier.

height_multiplier.help: Multiply the computed (post zero-value) height with this factor.

on.help: Whether the light is switched on.

mb.help: Whether motion-blur is active for this light. When set to true, the scene's illumination will correctly account for any blur() applied to the light's transformation matrix.

visible_in_camera.help: Whether the light is directly visible in the scene's active camera. When set to "use default" it reads from the value of SceneVariable lights_visible_in_camera.

color.help:
The light's RGB values.
These are combined multiplicatively with the intensity and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

intensity.help:
The light's intensity.
This is combined multiplicatively with the color and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

exposure.help:
The light's exposure value.
This value provides an alternative to the intensity value as a mechanism for controlling the light's overall brightness, and is inspired by the corresponding photographic term but is generalised to apply independently to each light. To calculate its effect, pow(2, exposure) is combined multiplicatively with the color and other attributes in determining the light's 3-channel radiance.

max_shadow_distance.help:
The distance from the light beyond which a light-receiving surface will no longer receive shadows cast from that light.
Note that the distance is thresholded for each occlusion ray cast for this light, it is possible for a receiving point to lie at an intermediate distance such that some parts of the light are closer than the threshold distance and other parts beyond it, in which case the point will appearto be in partial shadow.

min_shadow_distance.help:
The distance from the light before which a light-receiving surface will no longer receive shadows cast from that light.
Note that the distance is thresholded for each occlusion ray cast for this light, it is possible for a receiving point to lie at an intermediate distance such that some parts of the light are closer than the threshold distance and other parts beyond it, in which case the point will appearto be in partial shadow.

presence_shadows.help: Switch this attribute on for shadows cast from this light to correctly respect presence values. When off, surfaces with a material with presence less than 1.0 will cast opaque shadows from this light. This is an optimization - when the attribute is off, occlusion rays (fast) are used for testing for shadows. When it is on, regular rays (slower) are used, and the material's presence is evaluated to determine how much shadowing should occur. When set to "use default" it reads from the value of SceneVariable enable_presence_shadows.

ray_termination.help: Whether the light is used for ray termination color. Ray termination color is used for filling in falsely dark areas where ray paths have been terminated too early by the depth controls. Such a ray path immediately exits to any ray termination light(s) present in the light set being applied to the lobe, ignoring occlusion by scene geometry. Any light can either be a regular light or a ray termination light (but not both). Thus they can be freely assigned to light sets, which provides a mechanism for applying specific ray termination lights to specific materials, parts or objects. Ray termination color is only applied to non-hair transmission lobes.

texture_filter.help: The filtering mode to apply to the texture. Nearest neighbor is the cheapest filtering mode but produces a blocky result. Switch linear filtering on for a smoother result. Additionally, mip-mapping can be switched on with either nearest neighbor or linear filtering.

texture.help: File name of the texture applied to the light. If set to the empty string, no texture is applied. Any file format supported by OpenImageIO can be used. The texture is used in 2 ways - for looking up the texture value at the intersection point when a ray hits the light, and for building a lookup-table-based auxilliary data structure used for distributing light samples over the texture.

saturation.help:
Per-channel saturation used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = lerp(luminance(inputRGB), input, saturation).

contrast.help:
Per-channel contrast used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. The operation mimics Nuke's ColorCorrect node's contrast function:
  For input >  0, output = 0.18 * pow(inputCompnent/0.18, contrast).
  For input <= 0, output = 0.18 * input * pow(1/0.18, contrast).

gamma.help:
Per-channel gamma used in color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  For input >  0, output = pow(input, gamma)
  For input <= 0, output = input

gain.help:
Per-channel gain used in tandem with a per-channel offset for color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = input * gain + offset

offset.help:
Per-channel offset used in tandem with a per-channel gain for color-correcting the light's texture, if one is present. This is achieved by applying the following formula for each channel:
  output = input * gain + offset

temperature.help:
Color temperature using Nuke-style T/M/I settings (T = temperature, M = magenta/green, I = intensity). This is achieved as follows:
The 3-channel temperature is interpreted as the vector (T,M,I). The followiong scale values are then applied to the RGB components:
  outputR = inputR * (pow(2,I) + M/3 - T/2)
  outputG = inputG * (pow(2,I) - 2*M/3
  outputB = inputB * (pow(2,I) + M/3 + T/2)

texture_translation.help: Translation of the texture in (u,v)-space, in units of the texture size. For example, a translation of (0.25, 0.5) will translate the texture one-quarter of its width in the u-direction and one-half of its height in the v-direction.

texture_coverage.help: Texture scales in the u and v-directions.

texture_reps_u.help: Number of times texture repeats in u over the scaled texture space.

texture_reps_v.help: Number of times texture repeats in v over the scaled texture space.

texture_mirror_u.help: Whether to mirror the texture in the u-direction. If set to false, the texture is repeated in the u-direction.

texture_mirror_v.help: Whether to mirror the texture in the v-direction. If set to false, the texture is repeated in the v-direction.

texture_border_color.help: RGB value used when a texture lookup occurs outside the texture.

light_filters.help: Vector of LightFilters associated with the light.

label.help: Label used in light aov expressions.

visible_diffuse_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse reflection.

visible_diffuse_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in diffuse transmission.

visible_glossy_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy reflection.

visible_glossy_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in glossy transmission (refraction).

visible_mirror_reflection.help: Whether the light is visible in miror reflection.

visible_mirror_transmission.help: Whether the light is visible in miror transmission (refraction).

light.help: The light on which to use the portal.

normalized.help: When set to true, the size of the light can be changed without altering the amount of total energy cast into the scene. This is achieved via scaling the light's radiance by the reciprocal of its surface area. When set to false, the radiance is used as-is, regardless of surface area.

apply_scene_scale.help: Whether to apply scene scale variable when normalized.

width.help: The width of the rectangle, i.e. the size measured in the light's local x-direction.

height.help: The height of the rectangle, i.e. the size measured in the light's local y-direction.

spread.help: The directionality of light emission. 1 is completely diffuse hemisphere. 0 is parallel to normal of light.

sidedness.help: When set to 0 (regular), light is emitted from the front-facing surface of the disk. When set to 1 (reverse), light is emitted from the back-facing surface of the disk. When set to 2 (2-sided), light is emitted from both surfaces of the disk.

clear_radius.help: Shadows less than this distance from the light are ignored. Setting this value to 0.0 or less effectively disables this feature.

clear_radius_falloff_distance.help: Distance over which the shadows fall off. Shadows are fully visible at a distance clear_radius + clear_radius_falloff_distance from the light, and fully invisble at a distance clear_radius from the light.

clear_radius_interpolation_type.help: Interpolation type to use for the clear radius shadow falloff.

			Smooth: 4 | Catmull Rom: 5 | Monotone Cubic: 6

input.help: Input signal for ramp, used when ramp type is set to input

space.help: Only applies when 'texture coordinates' is set to 'default state coordinates'

color_space.help: Color space to perform interpolation in

texture_coordinates.help: Whether to read existing coordinates or bind custom ones

wrap_type.help: Whether to repeat the ramp or maintain the color at each edge

uv_wave.help: Creates waves which perturb the ramp pattern

uv_repeat.help: Number of times to repeat the ramp pattern

monochrome.help: outputs the same color for all three channels

extra_aovs.help: Bind this attribute to a 'ListMap' that contains references to ExtraAovMaps that specify additional outputs that can be assigned to a RenderOutput "light aov" result

label.help: label used in material and light aovs

priority.help: The material's place in an order of precedence for overlapping dielectrics. A value of 0 means the priority should be ignored. Materials with lower numbers (higher priority) "override" materials with higher numbers (lower priority).  To enable automatic removal of self-overlapping geometry, a non-zero priority must be set on the geometry's material.

record_reflected_cryptomatte.help: Indicates whether the next reflected surface should appear in the reflected cryptomatte layers

record_refracted_cryptomatte.help: Indicates whether the next refracted surface should appear in the refracted cryptomatte layers

default_material.help: The default material to use if no material was specified for the incoming ray type.

camera_ray_material.help: The material to use if the incoming ray is a camera ray.

indirect_mirror_ray_material.help: The material to use if the incoming ray is an indirect mirror ray.

indirect_glossy_ray_material.help: The material to use if the incoming ray is an indirect glossy ray.

indirect_diffuse_ray_material.help: The material to use if the incoming ray is an indirect diffuse ray.

cutout_camera_rays.help: If true, this material acts as a cutout material, i.e. we terminate any rays that encounter this material.

label.help: label used in material aov expresssions

references.help: list of geometries that geometry procedural can reference during procedural generate/update stages. For example, an instancer geometry procedural can instance primitives generated by the reference geometry procedural.

static.help: disable if the geometry will be updated between frames

side_type.help: set single sidedness of the mesh, will affect the visibility of the mesh based on normal direction

reverse_normals.help: enable to reverse the normals in the geometry

visible_in_camera.help: whether the geometry is visible to camera rays

visible_diffuse_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in diffuse reflection

visible_diffuse_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in diffuse transmission

visible_glossy_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in glossy reflection.

visible_glossy_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in glossy transmission (refraction).

visible_mirror_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in miror reflection.

visible_mirror_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in miror transmission (refraction).

visible_volume.help: whether the geometry is visible in indirect volume rays

ray_epsilon.help: When a secondary ray is fired, anything within this distance of the intersection point will be ignored.  Instead, it is considered part of the current intersection's geometry.  If zero, an automatically calculated epsilon will be used.

shadow_ray_epsilon.help: When a shadow ray is fired, anything within this distance of the intersection point will be ignored.  If this value is less than "ray_epsilon", then it has no additional effect.

shadow_receiver_label.help: Label used to associate Geometry objects into ShadowReceiverSets. Using this in combination with the ["shadow_exclusion_mappings"] attribute, shadows from specified geometries or their parts can be suppressed from casting shadows onto one or more specified sets.

shadow_exclusion_mappings.help:
A space-separated list of mappings of the form A:B where:
A is a comma-separated list of names of parts of this Geometry, or an asterisk to specify the whole geometry;
B is a comma-separated list of shadow receiver set labels established using the ["shadow_receiver_label"] attribute, or an asterisk to specify to all such sets in the scene.
For each of the listed mappings, shadows from the parts specified in A will be suppressed from casting onto any geometries in the ShadowReceiverSets specified in B.
**Note: no part name should appear more than once in the string, otherwise the behavior is undefined.**

contains_camera.help: Specifies whether the geometry contains the camera and should be used for IOR tracking. This should not be changed by the user -- they should instead attach the relevant geometry to the camera, which will then flag this geometry.

dicing_camera.help: Alternate camera that is used for adaptive tessellation.  This is useful if you want adaptive tessellation to behave consistently in a sequence, regardless of what the main camera is doing

use_local_motion_blur.help: Enables the local motion blur feature, which makes the geometry procedural responsible for handling all of the geometry's motion and allows for custom effects

use_local_camera_motion_blur.help: Enables experimental feature that also attempts to remove the camera blur in the local regions

vertex_list_0.help: List of vertex positions used by the curves at motion step 0

vertex_list_1.help: If the curves are in motion, the vertex positions for the second motion step are stored in this attribute

velocity_list_0.help: Optionally declared explicit vertex velocities to use instead of vertex positions from a second motion step'

velocity_list_1.help: Optionally declared second set of vertex velocities together with vertex positions from the second motion step for cubic motion interpolation

accleration_list.help: Optionally declared vertex accelerations for quadratic motion interpolation

radius_list.help: List of radius values.  The rate will be determined based on the number of values.   A single value will be interpreted as constant.   If the  number of values matches the number of curves, the rate will be interpreted as uniform.   If the number of values matches the number of vertices, the rate will be interpreted as  vertex.

use_screen_space_radius.help: Radius is applied in screen space

part_list.help: List of part names, used in conjunction with 'part_indices' to assign per-part materials

part_indices.help: List of part indices.  No index should have a value greater than the size of 'curves_vertex_count'

velocity_scale.help: Adjusts magnitude of velocity-based motion blur

uv_list.help: If the curves are using UVs, store them per-curve in this list

curve_type.help: Curve interpolation type is linear, bezier, or bspline

primitive_attributes.help: Vector of UserData.  Each key/value pair will be added as a primitive attribute of the curves.

tessellation_rate.help: Number of segments to split curve spans into

curves_subtype.help: Selects the style the curves are rendered

use_rotation_motion_blur.help: If "xform" is time varying and motion blur is enabled, enabling this feature can produce a curved rotation trail.  Enabling this feature will disable adaptive tessellation for this mesh

motion_blur_type.help:
Motion blur type for PolygonMesh/Points/Curves.
"static" will treat the mesh as static.
"velocity" will blur using the supplied vertex positions and velocities.
"frame delta" will interpolate between the two supplied vertex positions.
"acceleration" will blur using the supplied vertex positions, velocities and accelerations.
"hermite" will use supplied pair of positions and pair of velocities to interpolate along a cubic Hermite curve.
"best" will use choose the method which provides the highest quality given the available data.

curved_motion_blur_sample_count.help: Number of time samples generated along each curve when using curved motion blur

primitive_attribute_frame.help:
Which frame(s) do we take the primitive attributes from?
	O : first motion step
	1 : second motion step
	2 : both motion steps

method.help: Specifies the source of the transform data for instancing. If set to "xform attributes", data is used from the "positions", "orientations", "scales" attributes.If set to "xform list", data is used from the "xform list"attribute.

instance_level.help: Sets the level/depth of this instance.  This adds a Mat4f primitive attribute to the geometry which can be referenced during shading to use the local space of each instance.  The name of the primitive attribute corresponds the the instance level  that is set (i.e. "instance_level_0", "instance_level_1", etc)

xform_list.help: A list of Mat4 transforms that represent the per-instance xform.

positions.help: A list of Vec3 values that represent the per-instance position.

orientations.help: A list of Vec4 quaternions that represent the per-instance orientation. The length of the list should be either 0 or consistent with "positions".

scales.help: A list of Vec3 values that represet the per-instance velocity(motion blur).  The length of the list should be either 0 or consistent with "positions".

ref_indices.help: A list of index values to specify which reference geometry to instance at each   position.   The list corresponds to entries in the "references" attribute.  The length of the list should be either 0 or consistent with "positions"|"xform_list".  The index entry falls back to 0 when this attribute is empty or the value of entry is out of index range

disable_indices.help: A list of index values to hide / disable.  For example, with 4 instances you can supply a list of 0, 2 to disable those instances.  If an index in this list is out of range, it is ignored.

primitive_attributes.help: A list of UserData to specify arbitrary primitive attributes(For example, color or roughness multiplier) per-instance

evaluation_frame.help: Evaluate geometry at specified frame (relative) instead of SceneVariables frame.

use_reference_xforms.help: Transform the reference (prototype) geometry by it's node_xform parameter before applying the instance transform

use_reference_attributes.help: Use the geometry attributes of the reference (prototype) instead of the ones on the InstanceGeometry.   Currently only works for shadow_ray_epsilon

orientation.help: When set to "left-handed", normals are generated using the left-handed rule. This reverses the direction of generated normals, and which side of surfaces is considered the front, without affecting supplied normals.

part_list.help: List of part names, used in conjunction with 'part face count list' and 'part faces indicies' to assign per-part materials

part_face_count_list.help: List of the number of faces belonging to the part with corresponding index in 'part list'.

part_face_indices.help: List of face indices. No index should have a value greater than the size of 'face_vertex_count'

uv_list.help: If the mesh is using UVs, store them per face-vertex in this list

normal_list.help: If the mesh is using normals, store them per face-vertex in this list

velocity_scale.help: Adjusts magnitude of velocity-based motion blur

is_subd.help: If true, a SubdivisionMesh primitive will be created - PolygonMesh otherwise

subd_boundary.help: Boundary interpolation: Corners, Edges or None

subd_fvar_linear.help: Face-varying linear interpolation: None, Corners Only, Corners Plus 1 or Plus 2 (RenderMan), Boundaries, or All

subd_crease_indices.help: List of vertex index pairs for each crease edge with an assigned sharpness.

subd_crease_sharpnesses.help: Sharpness value for each crease edge.

subd_corner_indices.help: List of indices for each corner vertex with an assigned sharpness.

subd_corner_sharpnesses.help: Sharpness value for each corner vertex.

primitive_attributes.help: Vector of UserData.  Each key/value pair will be added as a primitive attribute of the mesh.

mesh_resolution.help: The maximum resolution to which a mesh will be tessellated. NOTE: to prevent t-junctions, only even values are supported. If "mesh resolution" is set to n, it will first be rounded up to N=n+1 for odd n, N=n for even n. An edge on an input face will then be tessellated to at most N segments. If "adaptive error" is set to 0, every edge on the input face will be uniformly tessellated to N segments. Otherwise the mesh will tessellated adaptively based on its relationship to the camera.

adaptive_error.help: The maximum allowable difference in pixels for subdivison mesh adaptive tessellation (each final tessellated edge won't be longer than n pixels if adaptive error is set to n). A value of 0 disables adaptive tessellation, reverting to uniform tessellation, which sometimes is more stable in animation. Adaptive tessellation is not supported for instances. The maximum tessellation won't exceed the value specified in mesh_resolution.  This prevents excessive tessellation when the mesh is extremely close to the camera.

smooth_normal.help: Generates smooth shading normals on a PolygonMesh when the mesh doesn't provide shading normals
