choice.help: which of the 64 inputs (0 to 63) to use

extra_aovs.help: Bind this attribute to a 'ListMap' that contains references to ExtraAovMaps that specify additional outputs that can be assigned to a RenderOutput "light aov" result

label.help: label used in material and light aovs

priority.help: The material's place in an order of precedence for overlapping dielectrics. A value of 0 means the priority should be ignored. Materials with lower numbers (higher priority) "override" materials with higher numbers (lower priority).  To enable automatic removal of self-overlapping geometry, a non-zero priority must be set on the geometry's material.

record_reflected_cryptomatte.help: Indicates whether the next reflected surface should appear in the reflected cryptomatte layers

record_refracted_cryptomatte.help: Indicates whether the next refracted surface should appear in the refracted cryptomatte layers

choice.help: which of the 64 inputs (0 to 63) to use
which of the 64 inputs (0 to 63) to use, values greater than 63 get cycled back to be in [0,63]

normal_map_output.help: When on, encodes the output to clamped [0, 1] in the same manner as a normal map

input.help: RenderOutput to transform into tangent space

N.help: Connect a RenderOutput with State N AOV here. Used to construct tangent space.

dPds.help: Connect a RenderOutput with State dPds AOV here. Used to construct tangent space.

mask.help: RenderOutput used to mask the output, revealing input1

invert_mask.help: Invert the value of the mask

input_diffuse.help: RenderOutput that represents diffuse reflection LPE

input_glossy.help: RenderOutput that represents glossy reflection LPE

input_albedo.help: RenderOutput that represents diffuse albedo material aov

num_cels.help: Sets number of toon cels in diffuse shading

ambient.help: Adds an ambient light to the cel shading

ink_depth_threshold.help: The threshold for the depth-based ink outline

ink_normal_threshold.help: The threshold for the normal-based ink outline

ink_normal_scale.help: Increase for a more pronounced normal-based ink outline

edge_detector.help: Type of edge detector to use

fill_color.help: Default color, within the outlines and creases

outline_color.help: Outlines are silhouettes of the geometry

crease_color.help: Creases are sharp edges like corners in the geometry.

outline_threshold.help: In most cases, the shader would trace an outline of a model well when this threshold is zero.

outline_scale.help: This attribute controls the thickness of outlines.

crease_threshold.help: This attribute sets the threshold angle (in degree units) to draw creases. The more the threshold angle is, the less the creases are traced.

crease_scale.help: This attribute controls the thickness of creases.

geometries.help: Geometry objects that are members of this TraceSet

parts.help: Part names (one for each geometry object)

input_normal.help: input normal in either tangent or render space

transform.help: transform to apply to the normals

decode_input_normal.help: decode the input normal if it's in tangent space [0,1] -> [-1,1]

input_type.help: the type of input value provided

concatenate_instance_level_transforms.help: When true, instance level transforms below the specified one are concatenated otherwise only the selected level's transform is used

camera.help: an alternate camera to use when transforming to/from 'camera' space

object.help: an alternate object to use when transforming to/from 'object' space

use_custom_window_coordinates.help: used to apply non-uniform scaling to projection

front_color.help: The color rendered on the front side of geometry

back_color.help: The color rendered on the back side of geometry

space.help: Determines source of coordinates. Default is mesh UVs. Other options procedurally create UVs from the chosen space.

scale.help: Scales UV coordinates (after rotation, before offset)

rotation_center.help: UV coordinate around which to rotate

rotation_axis.help: Axis in which to rotate (only for 3D spaces)

label.help: label used in material aov expresssions

references.help: list of geometries that geometry procedural can reference during procedural generate/update stages. For example, an instancer geometry procedural can instance primitives generated by the reference geometry procedural.

static.help: disable if the geometry will be updated between frames

side_type.help: set single sidedness of the mesh, will affect the visibility of the mesh based on normal direction

reverse_normals.help: enable to reverse the normals in the geometry

visible_in_camera.help: whether the geometry is visible to camera rays

visible_diffuse_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in diffuse reflection

visible_diffuse_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in diffuse transmission

visible_glossy_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in glossy reflection.

visible_glossy_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in glossy transmission (refraction).

visible_mirror_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in miror reflection.

visible_mirror_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in miror transmission (refraction).

visible_volume.help: whether the geometry is visible in indirect volume rays

ray_epsilon.help: When a secondary ray is fired, anything within this distance of the intersection point will be ignored.  Instead, it is considered part of the current intersection's geometry.  If zero, an automatically calculated epsilon will be used.

shadow_ray_epsilon.help: When a shadow ray is fired, anything within this distance of the intersection point will be ignored.  If this value is less than "ray_epsilon", then it has no additional effect.

shadow_receiver_label.help: Label used to associate Geometry objects into ShadowReceiverSets. Using this in combination with the ["shadow_exclusion_mappings"] attribute, shadows from specified geometries or their parts can be suppressed from casting shadows onto one or more specified sets.

shadow_exclusion_mappings.help:
A space-separated list of mappings of the form A:B where:
A is a comma-separated list of names of parts of this Geometry, or an asterisk to specify the whole geometry;
B is a comma-separated list of shadow receiver set labels established using the ["shadow_receiver_label"] attribute, or an asterisk to specify to all such sets in the scene.
For each of the listed mappings, shadows from the parts specified in A will be suppressed from casting onto any geometries in the ShadowReceiverSets specified in B.
**Note: no part name should appear more than once in the string, otherwise the behavior is undefined.**

contains_camera.help: Specifies whether the geometry contains the camera and should be used for IOR tracking. This should not be changed by the user -- they should instead attach the relevant geometry to the camera, which will then flag this geometry.

dicing_camera.help: Alternate camera that is used for adaptive tessellation.  This is useful if you want adaptive tessellation to behave consistently in a sequence, regardless of what the main camera is doing

use_local_motion_blur.help: Enables the local motion blur feature, which makes the geometry procedural responsible for handling all of the geometry's motion and allows for custom effects

use_local_camera_motion_blur.help: Enables experimental feature that also attempts to remove the camera blur in the local regions

prim_path.help: The geometry Prim to load from the USD Stage

use_prim_xform.help: Include the xform from the Prim during geometry creation

use_master_xform.help: If the prim is an instance proxy, use the xform from the master Prim during geometry creation

use_stage_cache.help:
Load the entire stage and use StageCache to share it among all UsdGeometry objects.
If this is false, load a stage masked to the prim path just for this UsdGeometry.
For large stages with thousands of unique assets, it is faster to enable the stage cache

subd_type.help:
PolygonMesh/SubdivisionMesh prim type to create.
"use mesh type" will use the type the Mesh prim specifies.
"force polygon mesh" will always resolve to PolygonMesh.
"force subdivision mesh" will always resolve to SubdivisionMesh.

radius_mult.help: Multiplier for the radius of points and curves

base_width_factor.help: Multiplier for the radius of the base of curves

tip_width_factor.help: Multiplier for the radius of the tip of curves

part_list.help:
List of part names.   The length of the
list should match the length of any "part" rate primitive attribute

primitive_attributes.help: A list of UserData scene objects specifying arbitrary primitive attributes

tessellation_rate.help: Number of segments to split curve spans into

curves_subtype.help: Selects the style the curves are rendered

mesh_resolution.help: The maximum resolution to which a mesh will be tessellated. NOTE: to prevent t-junctions, only even values are supported. If "mesh resolution" is set to n, it will first be rounded up to N=n+1 for odd n, N=n for even n. An edge on an input face will then be tessellated to at most N segments. If "adaptive error" is set to 0, every edge on the input face will be uniformly tessellated to N segments. Otherwise the mesh will tessellated adaptively based on its relationship to the camera.

adaptive_error.help: The maximum allowable difference in pixels for subdivison mesh adaptive tessellation (each final tessellated edge won't be longer than n pixels if adaptive error is set to n). A value of 0 disables adaptive tessellation, reverting to uniform tessellation, which sometimes is more stable in animation. Adaptive tessellation is not supported for instances. The maximum tessellation won't exceed the value specified in mesh_resolution.  This prevents excessive tessellation when the mesh is extremely close to the camera.

smooth_normal.help: Generates smooth shading normals on a PolygonMesh when the mesh doesn't provide shading normals

use_rotation_motion_blur.help: If "xform" is time varying and motion blur is enabled, enabling this feature can produce a curved rotation trail.  Enabling this feature will disable adaptive tessellation for this mesh

motion_blur_type.help:
Motion blur type for PolygonMesh/Points/Curves.
"static" will treat the mesh as static.
"velocity" will blur using the supplied vertex positions and velocities.
"frame delta" will interpolate between the two supplied vertex positions.
"acceleration" will blur using the supplied vertex positions, velocities and accelerations.
"hermite" will use supplied pair of positions and pair of velocities to interpolate along a cubic Hermite curve.
"best" will use choose the method which provides the highest quality given the available data.

curved_motion_blur_sample_count.help: Number of time samples generated along each curve when using curved motion blur

primitive_attribute_frame.help:
Which frame(s) do we take the primitive attributes from?
	O : first motion step
	1 : second motion step
	2 : both motion steps

use_evaluation_frame.help: Uses "evaluation frame" instead of SceneVariables frame

evaluation_frame.help: Evaluates geometry at the specified frame instead of SceneVariables frame

use_explicit_shading_attributes.help: Enable the use of explicit shading attributes (N, dPds, dPdt) if they are present

The name of the primitive attribute corresponds the the instance level  that is set (i.e. "instance_level_0", "instance_level_1", etc)

use_reference_xforms.help: Transform the reference (prototype) geometry by it's node_xform parameter before applying the instance transform

vec2f_values_0.help: vec2f type user data values for motion step 0

vec2f_values_1.help: vec2f type user data values for motion step 1

vec3f_key.help: key name for vec3f type user data

vec3f_values_0.help: vec3f type user data values for motion step 0

vec3f_values_1.help: vec3f type user data values for motion step 1

mat4f_key.help: key name for mat4f type user data

mat4f_values_0.help: mat4f type user data values for motion step 0

mat4f_values_1.help: mat4f type user data values for motion step 1

rate.help: The rate of the data.  Auto mode will guess the rate by comparing the number of values to component(i.e. part, face. vertex) counts.

label.help: label used in material aov expresssions

references.help: list of geometries that geometry procedural can reference during procedural generate/update stages. For example, an instancer geometry procedural can instance primitives generated by the reference geometry procedural.

static.help: disable if the geometry will be updated between frames

side_type.help: set single sidedness of the mesh, will affect the visibility of the mesh based on normal direction

reverse_normals.help: enable to reverse the normals in the geometry

visible_in_camera.help: whether the geometry is visible to camera rays

visible_diffuse_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in diffuse reflection

visible_diffuse_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in diffuse transmission

visible_glossy_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in glossy reflection.

visible_glossy_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in glossy transmission (refraction).

visible_mirror_reflection.help: whether the geometry is visible in miror reflection.

visible_mirror_transmission.help: whether the geometry is visible in miror transmission (refraction).

visible_volume.help: whether the geometry is visible in indirect volume rays

ray_epsilon.help: When a secondary ray is fired, anything within this distance of the intersection point will be ignored.  Instead, it is considered part of the current intersection's geometry.  If zero, an automatically calculated epsilon will be used.

shadow_ray_epsilon.help: When a shadow ray is fired, anything within this distance of the intersection point will be ignored.  If this value is less than "ray_epsilon", then it has no additional effect.

shadow_receiver_label.help: Label used to associate Geometry objects into ShadowReceiverSets. Using this in combination with the ["shadow_exclusion_mappings"] attribute, shadows from specified geometries or their parts can be suppressed from casting shadows onto one or more specified sets.

shadow_exclusion_mappings.help:
A space-separated list of mappings of the form A:B where:
A is a comma-separated list of names of parts of this Geometry, or an asterisk to specify the whole geometry;
B is a comma-separated list of shadow receiver set labels established using the ["shadow_receiver_label"] attribute, or an asterisk to specify to all such sets in the scene.
For each of the listed mappings, shadows from the parts specified in A will be suppressed from casting onto any geometries in the ShadowReceiverSets specified in B.
**Note: no part name should appear more than once in the string, otherwise the behavior is undefined.**

contains_camera.help: Specifies whether the geometry contains the camera and should be used for IOR tracking. This should not be changed by the user -- they should instead attach the relevant geometry to the camera, which will then flag this geometry.

dicing_camera.help: Alternate camera that is used for adaptive tessellation.  This is useful if you want adaptive tessellation to behave consistently in a sequence, regardless of what the main camera is doing

use_local_motion_blur.help: Enables the local motion blur feature, which makes the geometry procedural responsible for handling all of the geometry's motion and allows for custom effects

use_local_camera_motion_blur.help: Enables experimental feature that also attempts to remove the camera blur in the local regions

interpolation.help: Voxel interpolation to use when sampling the volume data

density_grid.help: The name of the density grid. If multiple grids have the same name, only the first grid with that name will be loaded. If a vdb file has multiple grids with the same name, you may use a suffix index to pick which grid to load, e.g. "density[3]". The index must be in [] brackets.

emission_grid.help: The name of the emission grid. If multiple grids have the same name, only the first grid with that name will be loaded. If a vdb file has multiple grids with the same name, you may use a suffix index to pick which grid to load, e.g. "emission[3]". The index must be in [] brackets.

velocity_grid.help: the name of vector grid representing the velocity field. Usually named "v" or "vel" in simulation export. If multiple velocity grids have the same name, only the first grid with that name will be loaded. If a vdb file has multiple grids with the same name, you may use a suffix index to pick which grid to load, e.g. "v[3]". The index must be in [] brackets. The index can be different from the index on the "density_grid".

velocity_scale.help: A scale factor for the velocity field. A value of 0 disables motion blur.

velocity_sample_rate.help: the relative scale of input velocity grid resolution. Lower value has lower memory overhead and lower fidelity of motion blur effect, which is sometimes desired for artistic reasons

emission_sample_rate.help: the relative scale of input emission grid resolution. Lower value has lower memory overhead and faster render time, with the cost of lower fidelity of emission shape and illumination

density_grid_name.help: The name of the grid within the .vdb file from which to sample for density(hint: use openvdb_print to see contents of .vdb file). If no grid is specified, it will use 'density' as the defaultIn cases where there are multiple grids with the same name, the grid name can be indexed (eg. density[1])

density_remap_input_min.help: Clamp the remapped input to this min value

density_remap_input_max.help: Clamp the remapped input to this max value

density_remap_output_min.help: Clamp the remapped output to this min value

density_remap_output_max.help: Clamp the remapped output to this max value

density_remap_inputs.help: List of input remap curve values

density_remap_outputs.help: List of output remap curve values

density_remap_interpolation_types.help: List of density remap interpolation types

vdb_interpolation_type.help: The type of interpolation to use when sampling the filter

blur_type.help: The type of blur to apply

color_tint.help: Tints the light filter.  Lower density increases the shift toward the tint color.

invert_density.help: Invert the density with density = 1 - density

bound_padding.help: bound padding defines how much to extend the bounding box of the object. Keep this value as low as possible unless the geometry skips tessellation because control cage bounding box is out of camera frustum but the displacement stretch out of the original object bounding box (pre-displacement). Setting the bound padding too large will consume more memory and tessellation time.

vector.help: The direction to apply the displacement.  Bind a map with tangent or object space vector values here.

factor.help: A multiplier applied to the input vector

source_space.help: The space that the map bound to the vector parameter is in

tangent_space_style.help: Controls how RGB maps to Tangent, Normal, and Bi-Normal
